.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:2:title: "Cognitive Subsystem"
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:35:The cognitive subsystem implements human memory principles to manage conversation context intelligently. It models how memories decay and strengthen through use, then transition between activity states based on research-validated algorithms from cognitive science and spaced repetition systems. Retrieval consumes `documentType` and `specLevel` metadata from indexing, so cognitive scoring is applied to both memory notes and spec documents.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:39:The cognitive subsystem is the "brain" of the memory system. It determines which memories stay active, which fade, and which get archived. Unlike simple time-based caching, it uses FSRS v4 power-law decay validated on 100M+ real human memory data.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:46:| Classification | 2       | ~960  | 5-state memory model + duplicate detection   |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:73:│  Archival Manager ──→ After 90 days → ARCHIVED                  │
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:89:| **5-State Model**            | HOT/WARM/COLD/DORMANT/ARCHIVED with thresholds                                        | Progressive memory transitions       |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:143:Memories transition through states based on retrievability:
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:151:| **ARCHIVED** | R < 0.02 OR 90+ days | Long-term storage | Not loaded    | 90+ days    |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:163:  ARCHIVED
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:166:[Any state] → HOT (R = 1.0) + Stability boost
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:171:Prevents duplicate memories using similarity thresholds:
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:175:| >= 0.95 (95%) | **REINFORCE**               | Exact duplicate, boost existing  |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:249:├── tier-classifier.ts          # 5-state model classifier (452 lines)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:338:**Purpose**: Progressive memory state transitions
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:351:const state = classifyState(0.65, 5);
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:352:// state = 'WARM' (R >= 0.25 but < 0.80)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:355:const state2 = classifyState(memory);
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:360:// classification = { state: 'WARM', retrievability: 0.65, effectiveHalfLife: 60 }
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:362:// Get memories filtered by state

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:371:// stats = { HOT: 3, WARM: 8, COLD: 12, DORMANT: 4, ARCHIVED: 20, total: 47 }
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:379:ARCHIVED_DAYS_THRESHOLD=90  # Default: 90 days
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:384:**Purpose**: Prevent duplicate memories with conflict detection

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:1016:Note: `temporal-contiguity.js` in dist/ is **not** orphaned. It is compiled from the active `temporal-contiguity.ts` source module. The `index.js` barrel file in dist/ may reference deleted modules and should be rebuilt.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:1046:| `HOT_THRESHOLD`           | 0.80    | Retrievability threshold for HOT state  |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:1047:| `WARM_THRESHOLD`          | 0.25    | Retrievability threshold for WARM state |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:1048:| `COLD_THRESHOLD`          | 0.05    | Retrievability threshold for COLD state |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/README.md:1049:| `ARCHIVED_DAYS_THRESHOLD` | 90      | Days inactive before archival           |

If not provided and mode=auto, intent is auto-detected from input.' }, specFolder: { type: 'string', description: 'Limit context to specific spec folder' }, tenantId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tenant boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, userId: { type: 'string', description: 'User boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, agentId: { type: 'string', description: 'Agent boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, sharedSpaceId: { type: 'string', description: 'Shared-space boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, limit: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, maximum: 100, description: 'Maximum results (mode-specific defaults apply)' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional server-issued session identifier for working-memory continuity. When provided, it must match an existing server-managed session or the call is rejected. Omit it to let the server generate a new session for this request.' }, enableDedup: { type: 'boolean', default: true, description: 'Enable session deduplication' }, includeContent: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Include full file content in results' }, includeTrace: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Include provenance-rich trace data (scores, source, trace) in results when underlying memory_search is called' }, tokenUsage: { type: 'number', minimum: 0.0, maximum: 1.0, description: "Optional caller token usage ratio (0.0-1.0)" }, anchors: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Filter content to specific anchors (e.g., ["state", "next-steps"] for resume mode)' }, profile: { type: 'string', enum: ['quick', 'research', 'resume', 'debug'], description: 'Optional response profile formatter. Returns a reduced or mode-aware response shape when profile formatting is enabled.' } }, required: ['input'] },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:77:        description: 'Session identifier for working memory and session deduplication (REQ-001). When provided with enableDedup=true, prevents duplicate memories from being returned in the same session (~50% token savings on follow-up queries).'
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:82:        description: 'Enable session deduplication (REQ-001). When true and sessionId provided, filters out already-sent memories.'
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:146:        description: 'Apply per-tier quantity limits to results. When true, enforces maximum counts per state tier to balance result diversity.'
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:150:        enum: ['HOT', 'WARM', 'COLD', 'DORMANT', 'ARCHIVED'],
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:152:        description: 'Minimum memory state to include in results. Memories below this state are filtered out. Order: HOT > WARM > COLD > DORMANT > ARCHIVED.'
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:173:        description: 'Include archived memories in search results. Default: false (archived excluded).'
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:193:  description: '[L2:Core] Simplified search — query + optional limit + optional spec folder. Delegates to memory_search with sensible defaults (intent auto-detect ON, dedup ON, content included, limit 10). Use this when you want fast search without configuring 31 parameters.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:213:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { prompt: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'User prompt or text to match against trigger phrases' }, specFolder: { type: 'string', description: 'Limit trigger matches to a specific spec folder' }, tenantId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tenant boundary for governed trigger matching.' }, userId: { type: 'string', description: 'User boundary for governed trigger matching.' }, agentId: { type: 'string', description: 'Agent boundary for governed trigger matching.' }, sharedSpaceId: { type: 'string', description: 'Shared-space boundary for governed trigger matching.' }, limit: { type: 'number', default: 3, minimum: 1, maximum: 100, description: 'Maximum number of matching memories to return (default: 3)' }, session_id: { type: 'string', description: 'Session identifier for cognitive features. When provided, enables attention decay and tiered content injection.' }, turnNumber: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, description: 'Current conversation turn number. Used with session_id for decay calculations.' }, include_cognitive: { type: 'boolean', default: true, description: 'Enable cognitive features (decay, tiers, co-activation). Requires session_id.' } }, required: ['prompt'] },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:218:  name: 'memory_save',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:219:  description: '[L2:Core] Index a memory file into the spec kit memory database. Reads the file, extracts metadata (title, trigger phrases), generates embedding, and stores in the index. Use this to manually index new or updated memory files. Includes pre-flight validation (T067-T070) for anchor format, duplicate detection, and token budget estimation. Token Budget: 3500.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:220:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { filePath: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'Absolute path to the memory file (must be in specs/**/memory/, .opencode/specs/**/memory/, specs/**/ for spec documents, or .opencode/skill/*/constitutional/)' }, force: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Force re-index even if content hash unchanged' }, dryRun: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Validate only without saving. Returns validation results including anchor format, duplicate check, and token budget estimation (CHK-160)' }, skipPreflight: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Skip pre-flight validation checks (not recommended)' }, asyncEmbedding: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'When true, embedding generation is deferred for non-blocking saves. Memory is immediately saved with pending status and an async background attempt is triggered. Default false preserves synchronous embedding behavior.' }, tenantId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tenant boundary for governed ingest.' }, userId: { type: 'string', description: 'User boundary for governed ingest.' }, agentId: { type: 'string', description: 'Agent boundary for governed ingest.' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Session boundary for governed ingest.' }, sharedSpaceId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional shared-memory space for collaboration saves.' }, provenanceSource: { type: 'string', description: 'Required provenance source when governance guardrails are enabled.' }, provenanceActor: { type: 'string', description: 'Required provenance actor when governance guardrails are enabled.' }, governedAt: { type: 'string', description: 'ISO timestamp for governed ingest. Defaults to now when omitted.' }, retentionPolicy: { type: 'string', enum: ['keep', 'ephemeral', 'shared'], description: 'Retention class applied to the saved memory.' }, deleteAfter: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional ISO timestamp after which retention sweep may delete the memory.' } }, required: ['filePath'] },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:233:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { folderRanking: { type: 'string', enum: ['count', 'recency', 'importance', 'composite'], description: 'How to rank folders: count (default, by memory count), recency (most recent first), importance (by tier), composite (weighted multi-factor score)', default: 'count' }, excludePatterns: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Regex patterns to exclude folders (e.g., ["z_archive", "scratch"])' }, includeScores: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Include score breakdown for each folder', default: false }, includeArchived: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Include archived/test/scratch folders in results', default: false }, limit: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, maximum: 100, description: 'Maximum number of folders to return', default: 10 } } },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:277:  name: 'memory_delete',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:292:  name: 'memory_update',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:294:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { id: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, description: 'Memory ID to update' }, title: { type: 'string', description: 'New title' }, triggerPhrases: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Updated trigger phrases' }, importanceWeight: { type: 'number', minimum: 0, maximum: 1, description: 'New importance weight (0-1)' }, importanceTier: { type: 'string', enum: ['constitutional', 'critical', 'important', 'normal', 'temporary', 'deprecated'], description: 'Set importance tier. Constitutional tier memories always surface at top of results.' }, allowPartialUpdate: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Allow update to succeed even if embedding regeneration fails. When true, metadata changes are applied and the embedding is marked for re-index. When false (default), the entire update is rolled back on embedding failure.' } }, required: ['id'] },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:328:  description: '[L5:Lifecycle] Create a named checkpoint of current memory state for later restoration. Token Budget: 600.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:364:  description: '[L5:Lifecycle] Restore memory state from a checkpoint. Token Budget: 600.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:403:  name: 'shared_space_upsert',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:478:  description: '[L6:Analysis] Capture epistemic state after task execution and calculate learning delta. Call after completing implementation work. Calculates Learning Index: LI = (KnowledgeDelta x 0.4) + (UncertaintyReduction x 0.35) + (ContextImprovement x 0.25). Token Budget: 1200.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:598:  description: '[L7:Maintenance] Get current state and progress for an async ingestion job.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:780:  name: 'deep_loop_graph_upsert',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:781:  description: '[L9:CoverageGraph] Idempotent upsert for coverage graph nodes and edges. Reducer writes graph deltas after each deep-loop iteration. Rejects self-loops, clamps weights to [0.0, 2.0], and merges metadata updates on repeated IDs. Requires specFolder, loopType, and sessionId for namespace isolation.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:796:            contentHash: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional content hash for deduplication' },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:802:        description: 'Nodes to upsert',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:818:        description: 'Edges to upsert (self-loops are rejected)',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:827:  description: '[L9:CoverageGraph] Structured analysis of deep-loop coverage graph state. Supports query types: uncovered_questions (questions with no coverage), unverified_claims (claims without verification), contradictions (CONTRADICTS edge pairs), provenance_chain (BFS from a node following citation/evidence edges), coverage_gaps (nodes missing incoming coverage edges), and hot_nodes (most connected nodes by edge count + weight).',

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:429:- `memory_save` (index new memories)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:539:- `resume`: Session recovery (loads previous state)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:555:- `minState`: Minimum memory state (HOT, WARM, COLD, DORMANT, ARCHIVED)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:563:### memory_save: Index a Memory File
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:565:`memory_save()` indexes a single new or updated memory file into the database. For bulk indexing, use `memory_index_scan()` instead.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:624:Every memory mutation (index, update, delete, force-reindex) appends a timestamped record to an immutable audit trail. No entry is ever overwritten or removed. Use the ledger to debug unexpected index state or audit what changed during a session.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:681:**Behind the scenes:** The AI calls `memory_context()` with `mode: "resume"` and `anchors: ["state", "next-steps"]`, then returns the previous session state.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:786:| `Cannot find module '@spec-kit/shared/...'` | Workspace dependency state is incomplete or stale | Run `npm install && npm run build` from the skill root |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:792:| Startup or resume shows graph `stale` | Freshness-aware startup detected drift before a structural read ran | Run a structural read to allow bounded inline repair, or run `code_graph_scan` for broader stale states |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:793:| Database appears stale after restore | Client still uses old MCP process with in-memory state | Fully restart OpenCode or Claude Code |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:890:After restoring a database backup or switching branches, the MCP server process may hold cached state from the previous database.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:907:3. **Stale build output after dependency changes.** The `dist/` folder was not rebuilt, or was rebuilt without the required workspace state.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:914:Use this procedure when an update leaves the server broken and you need to restore a working state.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:933:**Step 3: Reinstall and rebuild from a clean state**
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:1048:           memory_save, memory_index_scan, memory_stats
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:1065:| v1.x | 2025 | Adaptive fusion, extended telemetry, artifact-class routing, append-only mutation ledger, typed retrieval contracts. Semantic search, trigger matching, intent-aware context, session deduplication. |

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:2:title: "Spec Kit Memory - MCP Server"
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:59:| **Memory states** | 5 | HOT, WARM, COLD, DORMANT, ARCHIVED |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:74:| **Sessions** | Stateless | Working memory with attention decay, ~50% token savings via deduplication |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:77:| **Memory state** | Everything treated equally | 5-state cognitive lifecycle (HOT through ARCHIVED) with FSRS-driven transitions |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:78:| **Save quality** | Accept everything | 3-layer gate (structure, semantic sufficiency, duplicate) with dry-run preview |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:189:  "tool": "memory_save",
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:285:**Stage 4 -- Filter and annotate**. Enforces score immutability (no score changes after Stage 2). Applies state filtering by minimum state parameter. Annotates results with confidence labels (high/medium/low) and feature flag states.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:342:**Five cognitive states** based on access patterns:
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:344:**HOT** (just used) >> **WARM** (recently relevant) >> **COLD** (not accessed lately) >> **DORMANT** (inactive) >> **ARCHIVED** (stored but rarely surfaced)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:402:**Reconsolidation-on-save** -- handles near-duplicates intelligently. Nearly identical content gets merged. Contradictions retire the old version. Different content keeps both. Like a filing clerk who reads the new document, checks the cabinet and makes an informed decision instead of just stuffing it in.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:404:**Semantic sufficiency gating** -- rejects memories too thin or lacking real evidence. Short documents with strong structural signals (clear title, proper labels) get an exception.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:416:**SHA-256 content-hash deduplication** -- recognizes unchanged files instantly and skips expensive reprocessing.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:426:**Session deduplication** -- pushes down results you already saw. If you got a result 3 turns ago, new searches rank it lower. Saves approximately 50% of tokens on follow-up queries.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:465:**Mode-aware response profiles** -- formats results differently by situation. Quick lookup returns top answer only. Research returns full results with evidence. Resume returns state plus next-steps. Debug returns the full retrieval trace.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:507:**Phase 13 chunked-save finalization hardening** -- chunked saves now track the created parent and child IDs so finalization can stay transactional. Prediction-error supersede finalization records cross-path `supersedes` edges and marks predecessors superseded inside one transaction. Safe-swap updates now null old-child `parent_id` values before bulk delete inside that same finalization step, and any finalize failure triggers compensating cleanup that removes the staged replacement chunk tree. Parent BM25 mutation is delayed until at least one chunk succeeds and, for safe-swap updates, until finalization completes, which preserves the old parent BM25 state when all chunks fail.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:545:**Startup/recovery surfaces:** `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, and the startup brief now report freshness-aware graph status instead of count-only health. Startup surfaces are intentionally non-mutating snapshots, so later structural reads may still differ if repo state changes.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:578:| `sessionId` | string | Session ID for deduplication across turns |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:579:| `anchors` | string[] | Pull specific sections: `["state", "next-steps"]` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:612:Complete session bootstrap in one call. This is the canonical first-call recovery step on session start or after `/clear`. It wraps the full `session_resume` payload plus `session_health` and returns context, health, structural readiness and recommended next actions. Startup/bootstrap surfaces are freshness-aware but non-mutating; use `code_graph_scan` when readiness shows an empty or broad full-scan state.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:624:The main search tool. You type what you are looking for in plain language and the system searches through all stored knowledge to find the best matches. It understands meaning (beyond keywords), so searching for "login problems" can find a document titled "authentication troubleshooting."
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:637:| `minState` | string | Minimum state: `HOT`, `WARM`, `COLD`, `DORMANT`, `ARCHIVED` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:699:##### `memory_save`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:723:  "tool": "memory_save",
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:758:| `includeArchived` | boolean | Include ARCHIVED state memories in counts |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:789:##### `memory_delete`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:801:##### `memory_update`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:803:Change a memory's title, keywords or importance without deleting and re-creating it. When you change the title, the search index updates automatically. If the update fails partway through, everything rolls back to the way it was before.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:808:| `title` | string | Updated title |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:809:| `triggerPhrases` | string[] | Updated trigger phrases |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:811:| `importanceTier` | string | `constitutional`, `critical`, `important`, `normal`, `temporary`, `deprecated` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:848:Take a named snapshot of the current memory state. Like saving your game before a boss fight. Use before bulk operations or risky changes so you can restore if something goes wrong.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:891:##### `shared_space_upsert`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:926:Check the state of shared memory. See which spaces exist, who has access and whether the kill switch is active. Use this to verify your setup after making changes.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1222:├── core/                      # Runtime config, DB-state coordination, rebind hooks
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1247:| `handlers/memory-save.ts` | Runs the save pipeline: validates structure, checks dedup/quality gates, generates embeddings, and stores the result. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1332:    "anchors": ["decisions", "state", "next-steps"]
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1354:  "tool": "memory_save",
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1361:**What happens**: File is validated, embedded and indexed. Returns memory ID, quality score and duplicate check results.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1501:  "tool": "shared_space_upsert",
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1543:| Test a save without committing | `memory_save` | Set `dryRun: true` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1576:**Common causes**: Too little real content, missing required structure (headings, metadata) or semantic duplicate.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1581:{ "tool": "memory_save", "arguments": { "filePath": "/path/to/file.md", "dryRun": true } }
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1705:Yes, through shared memory. Call `shared_memory_enable`, create a space with `shared_space_upsert` and grant access with `shared_space_membership_set`. Spaces are deny-by-default and the first creator becomes owner. See [SHARED_MEMORY_DATABASE.md](../SHARED_MEMORY_DATABASE.md) for the full guide.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1721:**Q: What does the dryRun parameter do on memory_save?**
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1723:A dry run validates the file against the quality gate, estimates the token budget, checks for duplicates and returns a report, all without writing anything to the database. Use it to verify a file will pass before committing.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/schemas/README.md:53:**Shared validators** -- Reusable building blocks (`positiveInt`, `positiveIntMax`, `boundedNumber`, `intentEnum`, `importanceTierEnum`, `relationEnum`) keep per-tool schema definitions concise.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/schemas/README.md:55:**Discriminated delete** -- `memory_delete` uses a `z.union` of two branches: single-record delete (requires `id`) and bulk folder delete (requires `specFolder` + `confirm: true`).

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:2:title: "Search Subsystem"
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:119:**Score immutability invariant**: Stage 4 MUST NOT modify scores. Enforced via compile-time `Stage4ReadonlyRow` readonly fields and runtime `captureScoreSnapshot` / `verifyScoreInvariant` defence-in-depth. Applies memory-state filtering (HOT/WARM/COLD/DORMANT/ARCHIVED with per-tier limits), evidence gap detection (Z-score confidence check), quality floor (`QUALITY_FLOOR=0.005`), and token budget truncation.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:141:- **MMR Diversity**: Maximal Marginal Relevance reranking reduces near-duplicate results
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:347:| `session-state.ts`         | -      | TypeScript | Tracks per-session search state and retrieval context |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:348:| `session-transition.ts`    | -      | TypeScript | Handles session-state transition rules during retrieval flows |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:383:   MMR diversity reranking -> Reduce near-duplicate results
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:673:Processing steps applied during `memory_save` before a memory is persisted.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:678:**TM-02: Same-Path and Hash Dedup Hardening** (`save/dedup.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:679:Same-path `unchanged` only applies to healthy existing rows (`success`, `pending`, `partial`), so unhealthy embedding states still re-enter indexing. Cross-path content-hash dedup accepts chunked parents only when the parent row is in valid `partial` state and ignores invalid parent rows marked `complete`.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:687:| 2. Content Quality | 5-dimension weighted signal density | Dimensions: title (0.25), triggers (0.20), length (0.20), anchors (0.15), metadata (0.20) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:688:| 3. Semantic Dedup | Cosine similarity against existing memories | Threshold: 0.92 rejects near-duplicates |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:719:`buildBm25DocumentText()` builds the canonical lexical document from title, content, trigger phrases, and folder metadata. `normalizeLexicalQueryTokens()` is shared by BM25 and SQLite FTS flows so lexical matching stays aligned across search channels.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:891:- Deterministic test isolation improved (no shared mutable state leaks)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/chunk-reassembly.ts:79: * 1. Deduplicates by parent_id (keeps only the first/best-scoring chunk per parent)

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/README.md:54:| `lineage-state.ts` | Append-first lineage transitions, active projection reads, backfill, and as-of resolution |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/README.md:62:<!-- ANCHOR:implemented-state -->
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/README.md:65:- `incremental-index.ts` uses `file_mtime_ms`, `content_hash`, and `embedding_status` together, so unchanged-path fast paths still requeue rows whose embeddings are unhealthy.

   644				CREATE TRIGGER obs_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON observations BEGIN
   645					INSERT INTO observations_fts(rowid, title, content, tool_name, type, project, topic_key)
   646					VALUES (new.id, new.title, new.content, new.tool_name, new.type, new.project, new.topic_key);

   648	
   649				CREATE TRIGGER obs_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON observations BEGIN
   650					INSERT INTO observations_fts(observations_fts, rowid, title, content, tool_name, type, project, topic_key)
   651					VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.title, old.content, old.tool_name, old.type, old.project, old.topic_key);
   652				END;
   653	
   654				CREATE TRIGGER obs_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON observations BEGIN
   655					INSERT INTO observations_fts(observations_fts, rowid, title, content, tool_name, type, project, topic_key)
   656					VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.title, old.content, old.tool_name, old.type, old.project, old.topic_key);
   657					INSERT INTO observations_fts(rowid, title, content, tool_name, type, project, topic_key)

   690					VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.content, old.project);
   691					INSERT INTO prompts_fts(rowid, content, project)
   692					VALUES (new.id, new.content, new.project);
   693				END;
   694			`
   695			if _, err := s.execHook(s.db, promptTriggers); err != nil {
   696				return err
   697			}
   698		}
   699	
   700		return nil
   701	}
   702	
   703	func (s *Store) migrateFTSTopicKey() error {
   704		var colCount int
   705		err := s.db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pragma_table_xinfo('observations_fts') WHERE name = 'topic_key'").Scan(&colCount)
   706		if err != nil || colCount > 0 {
   707			return nil
   708		}
   709	
   710		if _, err := s.execHook(s.db, `
   711			DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS obs_fts_insert;
   712			DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS obs_fts_update;
   713			DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS obs_fts_delete;
   714			DROP TABLE IF EXISTS observations_fts;
   715			CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE observations_fts USING fts5(
   716				title,
   717				content,
   718				tool_name,
   719				type,
   720				project,
   721				topic_key,
   722				content='observations',
   723				content_rowid='id'
   724			);
   725			INSERT INTO observations_fts(rowid, title, content, tool_name, type, project, topic_key)
   726			SELECT id, title, content, tool_name, type, project, topic_key
   727			FROM observations
   728			WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
   729	
   730			CREATE TRIGGER obs_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON observations BEGIN
   731				INSERT INTO observations_fts(rowid, title, content, tool_name, type, project, topic_key)
   732				VALUES (new.id, new.title, new.content, new.tool_name, new.type, new.project, new.topic_key);
   733			END;
   734	
   735			CREATE TRIGGER obs_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON observations BEGIN
   736				INSERT INTO observations_fts(observations_fts, rowid, title, content, tool_name, type, project, topic_key)
   737				VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.title, old.content, old.tool_name, old.type, old.project, old.topic_key);
   738			END;
   739	
   740			CREATE TRIGGER obs_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON observations BEGIN
   741				INSERT INTO observations_fts(observations_fts, rowid, title, content, tool_name, type, project, topic_key)
   742				VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.title, old.content, old.tool_name, old.type, old.project, old.topic_key);
   743				INSERT INTO observations_fts(rowid, title, content, tool_name, type, project, topic_key)
   744				VALUES (new.id, new.title, new.content, new.tool_name, new.type, new.project, new.topic_key);
   745			END;
   746		`); err != nil {
   747			return fmt.Errorf("migrate fts topic_key: %w", err)
   748		}
   749		return nil
   750	}

   759			if err := s.createSessionTx(tx, id, project, directory); err != nil {

  1313	func (s *Store) DeleteObservation(id int64, hardDelete bool) error {
  1314		return s.withTx(func(tx *sql.Tx) error {
  1315			obs, err := s.getObservationTx(tx, id)
  1316			if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
  1317				return nil
  1318			}
  1319			if err != nil {
  1320				return err
  1321			}
  1322	
  1323			deletedAt := Now()
  1324			if hardDelete {
  1325				if _, err := s.execHook(tx, `DELETE FROM observations WHERE id = ?`, id); err != nil {
  1326					return err
  1327				}
  1328			} else {
  1329				if _, err := s.execHook(tx,
  1330					`UPDATE observations
  1331					 SET deleted_at = datetime('now'),
  1332					     updated_at = datetime('now')
  1333					 WHERE id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`,
  1334					id,
  1335				); err != nil {
  1336					return err
  1337				}
  1338				if err := tx.QueryRow(`SELECT deleted_at FROM observations WHERE id = ?`, id).Scan(&deletedAt); err != nil {
  1339					return err
  1340				}
  1341			}
  1342	
  1343			return s.enqueueSyncMutationTx(tx, SyncEntityObservation, obs.SyncID, SyncOpDelete, syncObservationPayload{
  1344				SyncID:     obs.SyncID,
  1345				Deleted:    true,
  1346				DeletedAt:  &deletedAt,
  1347				HardDelete: hardDelete,
  1348			})
  1349		})
  1350	}
  1351	
  1352	// ─── Timeline ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1353	//
  1354	// Timeline provides chronological context around a specific observation.
  1355	// Given an observation ID, it returns N observations before and M after,
  1356	// all within the same session. This is the "progressive disclosure" pattern
  1357	// from claude-mem — agents first search, then use timeline to drill into
  1358	// the chronological neighborhood of a result.
  1359	
  1360	func (s *Store) Timeline(observationID int64, before, after int) (*TimelineResult, error) {
  1361		if before <= 0 {
  1362			before = 5
  1363		}
  1364		if after <= 0 {
  1365			after = 5
  1366		}
  1367	
  1368		// 1. Get the focus observation
  1369		focus, err := s.GetObservation(observationID)
  1370		if err != nil {
  1371			return nil, fmt.Errorf("timeline: observation #%d not found: %w", observationID, err)
  1372		}
  1373	
  1374		// 2. Get session info
  1375		session, err := s.GetSession(focus.SessionID)
  1376		if err != nil {
  1377			// Session might be missing for manual-save observations — non-fatal
  1378			session = nil
  1379		}
  1380	
  1381		// 3. Get observations BEFORE the focus (same session, older, chronological order)
  1382		beforeRows, err := s.queryItHook(s.db, `
  1383			SELECT id, session_id, type, title, content, tool_name, project,
  1384			       scope, topic_key, revision_count, duplicate_count, last_seen_at, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
  1385			FROM observations
  1386			WHERE session_id = ? AND id < ? AND deleted_at IS NULL
  1387			ORDER BY id DESC
  1388			LIMIT ?
  1389		`, focus.SessionID, observationID, before)
  1390		if err != nil {
  1391			return nil, fmt.Errorf("timeline: before query: %w", err)
  1392		}
  1393		defer beforeRows.Close()
  1394	
  1395		var beforeEntries []TimelineEntry
  1396		for beforeRows.Next() {
  1397			var e TimelineEntry
  1398			if err := beforeRows.Scan(
  1399				&e.ID, &e.SessionID, &e.Type, &e.Title, &e.Content,
  1400				&e.ToolName, &e.Project, &e.Scope, &e.TopicKey, &e.RevisionCount, &e.DuplicateCount, &e.LastSeenAt,
  1401				&e.CreatedAt, &e.UpdatedAt, &e.DeletedAt,
  1402			); err != nil {
  1403				return nil, err
  1404			}
  1405			beforeEntries = append(beforeEntries, e)
  1406		}
  1407		if err := beforeRows.Err(); err != nil {
  1408			return nil, err
  1409		}
  1410		// Reverse to get chronological order (oldest first)
  1411		for i, j := 0, len(beforeEntries)-1; i < j; i, j = i+1, j-1 {
  1412			beforeEntries[i], beforeEntries[j] = beforeEntries[j], beforeEntries[i]
  1413		}
  1414	
  1415		// 4. Get observations AFTER the focus (same session, newer, chronological order)
  1416		afterRows, err := s.queryItHook(s.db, `
  1417			SELECT id, session_id, type, title, content, tool_name, project,
  1418			       scope, topic_key, revision_count, duplicate_count, last_seen_at, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
  1419			FROM observations
  1420			WHERE session_id = ? AND id > ? AND deleted_at IS NULL
  1421			ORDER BY id ASC
  1422			LIMIT ?
  1423		`, focus.SessionID, observationID, after)
  1424		if err != nil {
  1425			return nil, fmt.Errorf("timeline: after query: %w", err)
  1426		}
  1427		defer afterRows.Close()
  1428	
  1429		var afterEntries []TimelineEntry
  1430		for afterRows.Next() {
  1431			var e TimelineEntry
  1432			if err := afterRows.Scan(
  1433				&e.ID, &e.SessionID, &e.Type, &e.Title, &e.Content,
  1434				&e.ToolName, &e.Project, &e.Scope, &e.TopicKey, &e.RevisionCount, &e.DuplicateCount, &e.LastSeenAt,
  1435				&e.CreatedAt, &e.UpdatedAt, &e.DeletedAt,
  1436			); err != nil {
  1437				return nil, err
  1438			}
  1439			afterEntries = append(afterEntries, e)
  1440		}
  1441		if err := afterRows.Err(); err != nil {
  1442			return nil, err
  1443		}
  1444	
  1445		// 5. Count total observations in the session for context
  1446		var totalInRange int
  1447		s.db.QueryRow(
  1448			"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM observations WHERE session_id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL", focus.SessionID,
  1449		).Scan(&totalInRange)
  1450	
  1451		return &TimelineResult{
  1452			Focus:        *focus,
  1453			Before:       beforeEntries,
  1454			After:        afterEntries,
  1455			SessionInfo:  session,
  1456			TotalInRange: totalInRange,
  1457		}, nil
  1458	}
  1459	
  1460	// ─── Search (FTS5) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1461	
  1462	func (s *Store) Search(query string, opts SearchOptions) ([]SearchResult, error) {
  1463		// Normalize project filter so "Engram" finds records stored as "engram"
  1464		opts.Project, _ = NormalizeProject(opts.Project)
  1465	
  1466		limit := opts.Limit
  1467		if limit <= 0 {
  1468			limit = 10
  1469		}
  1470		if limit > s.cfg.MaxSearchResults {
  1471			limit = s.cfg.MaxSearchResults
  1472		}
  1473	
  1474		var directResults []SearchResult
  1475		if strings.Contains(query, "/") {
  1476			tkSQL := `
  1477				SELECT id, ifnull(sync_id, '') as sync_id, session_id, type, title, content, tool_name, project,
  1478				       scope, topic_key, revision_count, duplicate_count, last_seen_at, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
  1479				FROM observations
  1480				WHERE topic_key = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL
  1481			`
  1482			tkArgs := []any{query}
  1483	
  1484			if opts.Type != "" {
  1485				tkSQL += " AND type = ?"
  1486				tkArgs = append(tkArgs, opts.Type)
  1487			}
  1488			if opts.Project != "" {
  1489				tkSQL += " AND project = ?"
  1490				tkArgs = append(tkArgs, opts.Project)
  1491			}
  1492			if opts.Scope != "" {
  1493				tkSQL += " AND scope = ?"
  1494				tkArgs = append(tkArgs, normalizeScope(opts.Scope))
  1495			}
  1496	
  1497			tkSQL += " ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT ?"
  1498			tkArgs = append(tkArgs, limit)
  1499	
  1500			tkRows, err := s.queryItHook(s.db, tkSQL, tkArgs...)
  1501			if err == nil {
  1502				defer tkRows.Close()
  1503				for tkRows.Next() {
  1504					var sr SearchResult
  1505					if err := tkRows.Scan(
  1506						&sr.ID, &sr.SyncID, &sr.SessionID, &sr.Type, &sr.Title, &sr.Content,
  1507						&sr.ToolName, &sr.Project, &sr.Scope, &sr.TopicKey, &sr.RevisionCount, &sr.DuplicateCount,
  1508						&sr.LastSeenAt, &sr.CreatedAt, &sr.UpdatedAt, &sr.DeletedAt,
  1509					); err != nil {
  1510						break
  1511					}
  1512					sr.Rank = -1000
  1513					directResults = append(directResults, sr)
  1514				}
  1515			}
  1516		}
  1517	
  1518		// Sanitize query for FTS5 — wrap each term in quotes to avoid syntax errors
  1519		ftsQuery := sanitizeFTS(query)
  1520	
  1521		sqlQ := `
  1522			SELECT o.id, ifnull(o.sync_id, '') as sync_id, o.session_id, o.type, o.title, o.content, o.tool_name, o.project,
  1523			       o.scope, o.topic_key, o.revision_count, o.duplicate_count, o.last_seen_at, o.created_at, o.updated_at, o.deleted_at,
  1524			       fts.rank
  1525			FROM observations_fts fts
  1526			JOIN observations o ON o.id = fts.rowid
  1527			WHERE observations_fts MATCH ? AND o.deleted_at IS NULL
  1528		`
  1529		args := []any{ftsQuery}
  1530	
  1531		if opts.Type != "" {
  1532			sqlQ += " AND o.type = ?"
  1533			args = append(args, opts.Type)
  1534		}
  1535	
  1536		if opts.Project != "" {
  1537			sqlQ += " AND o.project = ?"
  1538			args = append(args, opts.Project)

exec

 succeeded in 0ms:
  1538			args = append(args, opts.Project)
  1539		}
  1540	
  1541		if opts.Scope != "" {
  1542			sqlQ += " AND o.scope = ?"
  1543			args = append(args, normalizeScope(opts.Scope))
  1544		}
  1545	
  1546		sqlQ += " ORDER BY fts.rank LIMIT ?"
  1547		args = append(args, limit)
  1548	
  1549		rows, err := s.queryItHook(s.db, sqlQ, args...)
  1550		if err != nil {
  1551			return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: %w", err)
  1552		}
  1553		defer rows.Close()
  1554	
  1555		seen := make(map[int64]bool)
  1556		for _, dr := range directResults {
  1557			seen[dr.ID] = true
  1558		}
  1559	
  1560		var results []SearchResult
  1561		results = append(results, directResults...)
  1562		for rows.Next() {
  1563			var sr SearchResult
  1564			if err := rows.Scan(
  1565				&sr.ID, &sr.SyncID, &sr.SessionID, &sr.Type, &sr.Title, &sr.Content,
  1566				&sr.ToolName, &sr.Project, &sr.Scope, &sr.TopicKey, &sr.RevisionCount, &sr.DuplicateCount,
  1567				&sr.LastSeenAt, &sr.CreatedAt, &sr.UpdatedAt, &sr.DeletedAt,
  1568				&sr.Rank,
  1569			); err != nil {
  1570				return nil, err
  1571			}
  1572			if !seen[sr.ID] {
  1573				results = append(results, sr)
  1574			}
  1575		}
  1576		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
  1577			return nil, err
  1578		}
  1579	
  1580		if len(results) > limit {
  1581			results = results[:limit]
  1582		}
  1583		return results, nil
  1584	}
  1585	
  1586	// ─── Stats ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1587	
  1588	func (s *Store) Stats() (*Stats, error) {
  1589		stats := &Stats{}
  1590	
  1591		s.db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions").Scan(&stats.TotalSessions)
  1592		s.db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM observations WHERE deleted_at IS NULL").Scan(&stats.TotalObservations)
  1593		s.db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_prompts").Scan(&stats.TotalPrompts)
  1594	
  1595		rows, err := s.queryItHook(s.db, "SELECT project FROM observations WHERE project IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL GROUP BY project ORDER BY MAX(created_at) DESC")
  1596		if err != nil {
  1597			return stats, nil
  1598		}
  1599		defer rows.Close()
  1600	
  1601		for rows.Next() {
  1602			var p string
  1603			if err := rows.Scan(&p); err == nil {
  1604				stats.Projects = append(stats.Projects, p)
  1605			}
  1606		}
  1607	
  1608		return stats, nil
  1609	}
  1610	
  1611	// ─── Context Formatting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1612	
  1613	func (s *Store) FormatContext(project, scope string) (string, error) {
  1614		sessions, err := s.RecentSessions(project, 5)
  1615		if err != nil {
  1616			return "", err
  1617		}
  1618	
  1619		observations, err := s.RecentObservations(project, scope, s.cfg.MaxContextResults)
  1620		if err != nil {
  1621			return "", err
  1622		}
  1623	
  1624		prompts, err := s.RecentPrompts(project, 10)
  1625		if err != nil {
  1626			return "", err
  1627		}
  1628	
  1629		if len(sessions) == 0 && len(observations) == 0 && len(prompts) == 0 {
  1630			return "", nil
  1631		}
  1632	
  1633		var b strings.Builder
  1634		b.WriteString("## Memory from Previous Sessions\n\n")
  1635	
  1636		if len(sessions) > 0 {
  1637			b.WriteString("### Recent Sessions\n")
  1638			for _, sess := range sessions {
  1639				summary := ""
  1640				if sess.Summary != nil {
  1641					summary = fmt.Sprintf(": %s", truncate(*sess.Summary, 200))
  1642				}
  1643				fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- **%s** (%s)%s [%d observations]\n",
  1644					sess.Project, sess.StartedAt, summary, sess.ObservationCount)
  1645			}
  1646			b.WriteString("\n")
  1647		}
  1648	
  1649		if len(prompts) > 0 {
  1650			b.WriteString("### Recent User Prompts\n")
  1651			for _, p := range prompts {
  1652				fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- %s: %s\n", p.CreatedAt, truncate(p.Content, 200))
  1653			}
  1654			b.WriteString("\n")
  1655		}
  1656	
  1657		if len(observations) > 0 {
  1658			b.WriteString("### Recent Observations\n")
  1659			for _, obs := range observations {
  1660				fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- [%s] **%s**: %s\n",
  1661					obs.Type, obs.Title, truncate(obs.Content, 300))
  1662			}
  1663			b.WriteString("\n")
  1664		}
  1665	
  1666		return b.String(), nil
  1667	}
  1668

  1704		}

  2921	
  2922	func (s *Store) applyObservationDeleteTx(tx *sql.Tx, payload syncObservationPayload) error {
  2923		existing, err := s.getObservationBySyncIDTx(tx, payload.SyncID, true)
  2924		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
  2925			return nil
  2926		}
  2927		if err != nil {
  2928			return err
  2929		}
  2930		if payload.HardDelete {
  2931			_, err = s.execHook(tx, `DELETE FROM observations WHERE id = ?`, existing.ID)
  2932			return err
  2933		}
  2934		deletedAt := payload.DeletedAt
  2935		if deletedAt == nil {
  2936			now := Now()
  2937			deletedAt = &now
  2938		}
  2939		_, err = s.execHook(tx,
  2940			`UPDATE observations SET deleted_at = ?, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?`,
  2941			deletedAt, existing.ID,
  2942		)
  2943		return err
  2944	}
  2945	
  2946	func (s *Store) applyPromptUpsertTx(tx *sql.Tx, payload syncPromptPayload) error {

  2952				payload.SyncID, payload.SessionID, payload.Content, payload.Project,
  2953			)
  2954			return err
  2955		}

  3353	func newSyncID(prefix string) string {
  3354		b := make([]byte, 8)
  3355		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
  3356			return fmt.Sprintf("%s-%d", prefix, time.Now().UTC().UnixNano())
  3357		}
  3358		return prefix + "-" + hex.EncodeToString(b)
  3359	}
  3360	
  3361	func normalizeExistingSyncID(existing, prefix string) string {
  3362		if strings.TrimSpace(existing) != "" {
  3363			return existing
  3364		}
  3365		return newSyncID(prefix)
  3366	}
  3367	
  3368	// privateTagRegex matches <private>...</private> tags and their contents.
  3369	// Supports multiline and nested content. Case-insensitive.
  3370	var privateTagRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`(?is)<private>.*?</private>`)
  3371	
  3372	// stripPrivateTags removes all <private>...</private> content from a string.
  3373	// This ensures sensitive information (API keys, passwords, personal data)
  3374	// is never persisted to the memory database.
  3375	func stripPrivateTags(s string) string {
  3376		result := privateTagRegex.ReplaceAllString(s, "[REDACTED]")
  3377		// Clean up multiple consecutive [REDACTED] and excessive whitespace
  3378		result = strings.TrimSpace(result)
  3379		return result
  3380	}
  3381	
  3382	// sanitizeFTS wraps each word in quotes so FTS5 doesn't choke on special chars.
  3383	// "fix auth bug" → `"fix" "auth" "bug"`
  3384	func sanitizeFTS(query string) string {
  3385		words := strings.Fields(query)
  3386		for i, w := range words {
  3387			// Strip existing quotes to avoid double-quoting
  3388			w = strings.Trim(w, `"`)
  3389			words[i] = `"` + w + `"`
  3390		}
  3391		return strings.Join(words, " ")
  3392	}
  3393	
  3394	// ─── Passive Capture ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3395	
  3396	// PassiveCaptureParams holds the input for passive memory capture.
  3397	type PassiveCaptureParams struct {
  3398		SessionID string `json:"session_id"`
  3399		Content   string `json:"content"`
  3400		Project   string `json:"project,omitempty"`
  3401		Source    string `json:"source,omitempty"` // e.g. "subagent-stop", "session-end"
  3402	}
  3403	
  3404	// PassiveCaptureResult holds the output of passive memory capture.
  3405	type PassiveCaptureResult struct {
  3406		Extracted  int `json:"extracted"`  // Total learnings found in text
  3407		Saved      int `json:"saved"`      // New observations created
  3408		Duplicates int `json:"duplicates"` // Skipped because already existed
  3409	}
  3410	
  3411	// learningHeaderPattern matches section headers for learnings in both English and Spanish.
  3412	var learningHeaderPattern = regexp.MustCompile(
  3413		`(?im)^#{2,3}\s+(?:Aprendizajes(?:\s+Clave)?|Key\s+Learnings?|Learnings?):?\s*$`,
  3414	)
  3415	
  3416	const (
  3417		minLearningLength = 20
  3418		minLearningWords  = 4
  3419	)
  3420	
  3421	// ExtractLearnings parses structured learning items from text.
  3422	// It looks for sections like "## Key Learnings:" or "## Aprendizajes Clave:"
  3423	// and extracts numbered (1. text) or bullet (- text) items.
  3424	// Returns learnings from the LAST matching section (most recent output).
  3425	func ExtractLearnings(text string) []string {
  3426		matches := learningHeaderPattern.FindAllStringIndex(text, -1)
  3427		if len(matches) == 0 {
  3428			return nil
  3429		}
  3430	
  3431		// Process sections in reverse — use first valid one (most recent)
  3432		for i := len(matches) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
  3433			sectionStart := matches[i][1]
  3434			sectionText := text[sectionStart:]
  3435	
  3436			// Cut off at next major section header
  3437			if nextHeader := regexp.MustCompile(`\n#{1,3} `).FindStringIndex(sectionText); nextHeader != nil {
  3438				sectionText = sectionText[:nextHeader[0]]
  3439			}
  3440	
  3441			var learnings []string
  3442	
  3443			// Try numbered items: "1. text" or "1) text"
  3444			numbered := regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^\s*\d+[.)]\s+(.+)`).FindAllStringSubmatch(sectionText, -1)
  3445			if len(numbered) > 0 {
  3446				for _, m := range numbered {
  3447					cleaned := cleanMarkdown(m[1])
  3448					if len(cleaned) >= minLearningLength && len(strings.Fields(cleaned)) >= minLearningWords {
  3449						learnings = append(learnings, cleaned)
  3450					}
  3451				}
  3452			}
  3453	
  3454			// Fall back to bullet items: "- text" or "* text"
  3455			if len(learnings) == 0 {
  3456				bullets := regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^\s*[-*]\s+(.+)`).FindAllStringSubmatch(sectionText, -1)
  3457				for _, m := range bullets {
  3458					cleaned := cleanMarkdown(m[1])
  3459					if len(cleaned) >= minLearningLength && len(strings.Fields(cleaned)) >= minLearningWords {
  3460						learnings = append(learnings, cleaned)
  3461					}
  3462				}
  3463			}
  3464	
  3465			if len(learnings) > 0 {
  3466				return learnings
  3467			}
  3468		}
  3469	
  3470		return nil
  3471	}
  3472	
  3473	// cleanMarkdown strips basic markdown formatting and collapses whitespace.
  3474	func cleanMarkdown(text string) string {
  3475		text = regexp.MustCompile(`\*\*([^*]+)\*\*`).ReplaceAllString(text, "$1") // bold
  3476		text = regexp.MustCompile("`([^`]+)`").ReplaceAllString(text, "$1")       // inline code
  3477		text = regexp.MustCompile(`\*([^*]+)\*`).ReplaceAllString(text, "$1")     // italic
  3478		return strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(strings.Fields(text), " "))
  3479	}
  3480	
  3481	// PassiveCapture extracts learnings from text and saves them as observations.
  3482	// It deduplicates against existing observations using content hash matching.
  3483	func (s *Store) PassiveCapture(p PassiveCaptureParams) (*PassiveCaptureResult, error) {
  3484		// Normalize project name before storing
  3485		p.Project, _ = NormalizeProject(p.Project)
  3486	
  3487		result := &PassiveCaptureResult{}
  3488	
  3489		learnings := ExtractLearnings(p.Content)
  3490		result.Extracted = len(learnings)
  3491	
  3492		if len(learnings) == 0 {
  3493			return result, nil
  3494		}
  3495	
  3496		for _, learning := range learnings {
  3497			// Check if this learning already exists (by content hash) within this project
  3498			normHash := hashNormalized(learning)
  3499			var existingID int64
  3500			err := s.db.QueryRow(
  3501				`SELECT id FROM observations
  3502				 WHERE normalized_hash = ?
  3503				   AND ifnull(project, '') = ifnull(?, '')
  3504				   AND deleted_at IS NULL
  3505				 LIMIT 1`,
  3506				normHash, nullableString(p.Project),
  3507			).Scan(&existingID)
  3508	
  3509			if err == nil {
  3510				// Already exists — skip
  3511				result.Duplicates++
  3512				continue
  3513			}
  3514	
  3515			// Truncate for title: first 60 chars
  3516			title := learning
  3517			if len(title) > 60 {
  3518				title = title[:60] + "..."
  3519			}
  3520	
  3521			_, err = s.AddObservation(AddObservationParams{
  3522				SessionID: p.SessionID,
  3523				Type:      "passive",
  3524				Title:     title,
  3525				Content:   learning,
  3526				Project:   p.Project,
  3527				Scope:     "project",
  3528				ToolName:  p.Source,

codex
I’ve confirmed the core Engram hygiene path: schema-level counters and `deleted_at`, `AddObservation()` topic-key upsert before duplicate-window dedupe, soft-delete plus sync tombstones, and sync-import revival that clears `deleted_at`. I’m checking whether there’s any real garbage-collection path or restore semantics beyond that, and then I’ll map the closest Public surfaces for dedup, revision history, and archival behavior.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/README.md:3:description: "Decomposed pipeline modules for the memory_save MCP tool handler, covering dedup, embedding, PE gating, record creation, reconsolidation and response assembly."
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/README.md:27:`handlers/save/` contains the decomposed pipeline for the `memory_save` MCP tool. Each file owns a single stage of the save flow, from deduplication through embedding generation, save quality gating, prediction-error gating, reconsolidation, record creation, post-insert enrichment, and final response assembly.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/README.md:39:| `dedup.ts`                  | Pre-save deduplication. `checkExistingRow` matches by file path but returns `unchanged` only for healthy rows (`success`, `pending`, `partial`); `checkContentHashDedup` matches by content hash and accepts chunked parents only when their status is valid `partial`. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/README.md:42:| `reconsolidation-bridge.ts` | TM-06 reconsolidation bridge. When enabled and a checkpoint exists, delegates to the reconsolidation engine for merge/conflict resolution before falling through to normal creation. |

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/ops/README.md:32:- **File Watcher** monitors spec folders for Markdown file changes and triggers debounced re-indexing with content-hash deduplication.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/ops/README.md:43:| `file-watcher.ts` | Chokidar-based file watcher that detects add/change/unlink events on Markdown files, debounces them, compares content hashes to skip no-op writes and triggers re-indexing with bounded concurrency (max 2 parallel). Includes dotfile filtering, symlink-escape prevention and per-file AbortController cancellation. |

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:119:**Score immutability invariant**: Stage 4 MUST NOT modify scores. Enforced via compile-time `Stage4ReadonlyRow` readonly fields and runtime `captureScoreSnapshot` / `verifyScoreInvariant` defence-in-depth. Applies memory-state filtering (HOT/WARM/COLD/DORMANT/ARCHIVED with per-tier limits), evidence gap detection (Z-score confidence check), quality floor (`QUALITY_FLOOR=0.005`), and token budget truncation.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:678:**TM-02: Same-Path and Hash Dedup Hardening** (`save/dedup.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:679:Same-path `unchanged` only applies to healthy existing rows (`success`, `pending`, `partial`), so unhealthy embedding states still re-enter indexing. Cross-path content-hash dedup accepts chunked parents only when the parent row is in valid `partial` state and ignores invalid parent rows marked `complete`.

  2520	func (s *Store) PruneProject(project string) (*PruneResult, error) {
  2521		if project == "" {
  2522			return nil, fmt.Errorf("project name must not be empty")
  2523		}
  2524	
  2525		// Safety check: refuse to prune if observations exist.
  2526		count, err := s.CountObservationsForProject(project)
  2527		if err != nil {
  2528			return nil, fmt.Errorf("count observations: %w", err)
  2529		}
  2530		if count > 0 {
  2531			return nil, fmt.Errorf("project %q still has %d observations — cannot prune", project, count)
  2532		}
  2533	
  2534		result := &PruneResult{Project: project}
  2535	
  2536		err = s.withTx(func(tx *sql.Tx) error {
  2537			res, err := s.execHook(tx, `DELETE FROM user_prompts WHERE project = ?`, project)
  2538			if err != nil {
  2539				return fmt.Errorf("prune prompts: %w", err)
  2540			}
  2541			result.PromptsDeleted, _ = res.RowsAffected()
  2542	
  2543			res, err = s.execHook(tx, `DELETE FROM sessions WHERE project = ?`, project)
  2544			if err != nil {
  2545				return fmt.Errorf("prune sessions: %w", err)
  2546			}
  2547			result.SessionsDeleted, _ = res.RowsAffected()
  2548	
  2549			return nil
  2550		})
  2551		if err != nil {
  2552			return nil, err
  2553		}
  2554	
  2555		return result, nil
  2556	}
  2557	
  2558	// ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  2559	
  2560	func (s *Store) withTx(fn func(tx *sql.Tx) error) error {

 succeeded in 0ms:
  3198	// SuggestTopicKey generates a stable topic key suggestion from type/title/content.
  3199	// It infers a topic family (e.g. architecture/*, bug/*) and then appends
  3200	// a normalized segment from title/content for stable cross-session keys.
  3201	func SuggestTopicKey(typ, title, content string) string {
  3202		family := inferTopicFamily(typ, title, content)
  3203		cleanTitle := stripPrivateTags(title)
  3204		segment := normalizeTopicSegment(cleanTitle)
  3205	
  3206		if segment == "" {
  3207			cleanContent := stripPrivateTags(content)
  3208			words := strings.Fields(strings.ToLower(cleanContent))
  3209			if len(words) > 8 {
  3210				words = words[:8]
  3211			}
  3212			segment = normalizeTopicSegment(strings.Join(words, " "))
  3213		}
  3214	
  3215		if segment == "" {
  3216			segment = "general"
  3217		}
  3218	
  3219		if strings.HasPrefix(segment, family+"-") {
  3220			segment = strings.TrimPrefix(segment, family+"-")
  3221		}
  3222		if segment == "" || segment == family {
  3223			segment = "general"
  3224		}
  3225	
  3226		return family + "/" + segment
  3227	}
  3228	
  3229	func inferTopicFamily(typ, title, content string) string {
  3230		t := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(typ))
  3231		switch t {
  3232		case "architecture", "design", "adr", "refactor":
  3233			return "architecture"
  3234		case "bug", "bugfix", "fix", "incident", "hotfix":
  3235			return "bug"
  3236		case "decision":
  3237			return "decision"
  3238		case "pattern", "convention", "guideline":
  3239			return "pattern"
  3240		case "config", "setup", "infra", "infrastructure", "ci":
  3241			return "config"
  3242		case "discovery", "investigation", "root_cause", "root-cause":
  3243			return "discovery"
  3244		case "learning", "learn":
  3245			return "learning"
  3246		case "session_summary":
  3247			return "session"
  3248		}
  3249	
  3250		text := strings.ToLower(title + " " + content)
  3251		if hasAny(text, "bug", "fix", "panic", "error", "crash", "regression", "incident", "hotfix") {
  3252			return "bug"
  3253		}
  3254		if hasAny(text, "architecture", "design", "adr", "boundary", "hexagonal", "refactor") {
  3255			return "architecture"
  3256		}
  3257		if hasAny(text, "decision", "tradeoff", "chose", "choose", "decide") {
  3258			return "decision"
  3259		}
  3260		if hasAny(text, "pattern", "convention", "naming", "guideline") {
  3261			return "pattern"
  3262		}
  3263		if hasAny(text, "config", "setup", "environment", "env", "docker", "pipeline") {
  3264			return "config"
  3265		}
  3266		if hasAny(text, "discovery", "investigate", "investigation", "found", "root cause") {
  3267			return "discovery"
  3268		}
  3269		if hasAny(text, "learned", "learning") {
  3270			return "learning"
  3271		}
  3272	
  3273		if t != "" && t != "manual" {
  3274			return normalizeTopicSegment(t)
  3275		}
  3276	
  3277		return "topic"
  3278	}
  3279	
  3280	func hasAny(text string, words ...string) bool {
  3281		for _, w := range words {
  3282			if strings.Contains(text, w) {
  3283				return true
  3284			}
  3285		}
  3286		return false
  3287	}

    82	// ResolveTools takes a comma-separated string of profile names and/or
    83	// individual tool names and returns the set of tool names to register.
    84	// An empty input means "all" — every tool is registered.
    85	func ResolveTools(input string) map[string]bool {
    86		input = strings.TrimSpace(input)
    87		if input == "" || input == "all" {
    88			return nil // nil means register everything
    89		}
    90	
    91		result := make(map[string]bool)
    92		for _, token := range strings.Split(input, ",") {
    93			token = strings.TrimSpace(token)
    94			if token == "" {
    95				continue
    96			}
    97			if token == "all" {
    98				return nil
    99			}
   100			if profile, ok := Profiles[token]; ok {
   101				for tool := range profile {
   102					result[tool] = true
   103				}
   104			} else {
   105				// Treat as individual tool name
   106				result[token] = true
   107			}
   108		}

   210					mcp.WithDescription(`Save an important observation to persistent memory. Call this PROACTIVELY after completing significant work — don't wait to be asked.
   211	
   212	WHEN to save (call this after each of these):
   213	- Architectural decisions or tradeoffs
   214	- Bug fixes (what was wrong, why, how you fixed it)
   215	- New patterns or conventions established

   219	
   220	FORMAT for content — use this structured format:
   221	  **What**: [concise description of what was done]

   223	  **Where**: [files/paths affected, e.g. src/auth/middleware.ts, internal/store/store.go]
   224	  **Learned**: [any gotchas, edge cases, or decisions made — omit if none]

   226	TITLE should be short and searchable, like: "JWT auth middleware", "FTS5 query sanitization", "Fixed N+1 in user list"

   229	  title: "Switched from sessions to JWT"
   230	  type: "decision"

   232	
   233	  title: "Fixed FTS5 syntax error on special chars"
   234	  type: "bugfix"
   235	  content: "**What**: Wrapped each search term in quotes before passing to FTS5 MATCH\n**Why**: Users typing queries like 'fix auth bug' would crash because FTS5 interprets special chars as operators\n**Where**: internal/store/store.go — sanitizeFTS() function\n**Learned**: FTS5 MATCH syntax is NOT the same as LIKE — always sanitize user input"`),

   332					mcp.WithDeferLoading(true),
   333					mcp.WithTitleAnnotation("Delete Memory"),
   334					mcp.WithReadOnlyHintAnnotation(false),
   335					mcp.WithDestructiveHintAnnotation(true),
   336					mcp.WithIdempotentHintAnnotation(false),
   337					mcp.WithOpenWorldHintAnnotation(false),
   338					mcp.WithNumber("id",
   339						mcp.Required(),
   340						mcp.Description("Observation ID to delete"),
   740				TopicKey:  topicKey,
   741			})
   742			if err != nil {
   743				return mcp.NewToolResultError("Failed to save: " + err.Error()), nil
   744			}
   745	
   746			msg := fmt.Sprintf("Memory saved: %q (%s)", title, typ)
   747			if topicKey == "" && suggestedTopicKey != "" {
   748				msg += fmt.Sprintf("\nSuggested topic_key: %s", suggestedTopicKey)
   749			}
   750			if truncated {
   751				msg += fmt.Sprintf("\n⚠ WARNING: Content was truncated from %d to %d chars. Consider splitting into smaller observations.", len(content), s.MaxObservationLength())
   752			}
   753			if normWarning != "" {
   754				msg += "\n" + normWarning
   755			}
   756			if similarWarning != "" {
   757				msg += "\n" + similarWarning
   758			}
   759			return mcp.NewToolResultText(msg), nil
   760		}
   761	}
   762	
   763	func handleSuggestTopicKey() server.ToolHandlerFunc {
   764		return func(ctx context.Context, req mcp.CallToolRequest) (*mcp.CallToolResult, error) {
   765			typ, _ := req.GetArguments()["type"].(string)
   766			title, _ := req.GetArguments()["title"].(string)
   767			content, _ := req.GetArguments()["content"].(string)
   768	
   769			if strings.TrimSpace(title) == "" && strings.TrimSpace(content) == "" {
   770				return mcp.NewToolResultError("provide title or content to suggest a topic_key"), nil
   771			}
   772	
   773			topicKey := suggestTopicKey(typ, title, content)
   774			if topicKey == "" {
   775				return mcp.NewToolResultError("could not suggest topic_key from input"), nil
   776			}
   777	
   778			return mcp.NewToolResultText(fmt.Sprintf("Suggested topic_key: %s", topicKey)), nil
   779		}
   780	}
   781	
   782	func handleUpdate(s *store.Store) server.ToolHandlerFunc {
   783		return func(ctx context.Context, req mcp.CallToolRequest) (*mcp.CallToolResult, error) {
   784			id := int64(intArg(req, "id", 0))
   785			if id == 0 {
   786				return mcp.NewToolResultError("id is required"), nil
   787			}
   788	
   789			update := store.UpdateObservationParams{}
   790			if v, ok := req.GetArguments()["title"].(string); ok {
   820				return mcp.NewToolResultError("Failed to update memory: " + err.Error()), nil
   821			}
   822	
   823			msg := fmt.Sprintf("Memory updated: #%d %q (%s, scope=%s)", obs.ID, obs.Title, obs.Type, obs.Scope)
   824			if contentLen > s.MaxObservationLength() {
   825				msg += fmt.Sprintf("\n⚠ WARNING: Content was truncated from %d to %d chars. Consider splitting into smaller observations.", contentLen, s.MaxObservationLength())
   826			}
   827			return mcp.NewToolResultText(msg), nil
   828		}
   829	}
   830	
   831	func handleDelete(s *store.Store) server.ToolHandlerFunc {
   832		return func(ctx context.Context, req mcp.CallToolRequest) (*mcp.CallToolResult, error) {
   833			id := int64(intArg(req, "id", 0))
   834			if id == 0 {
   835				return mcp.NewToolResultError("id is required"), nil
   836			}
   837	
   838			hardDelete := boolArg(req, "hard_delete", false)
   839			if err := s.DeleteObservation(id, hardDelete); err != nil {
   840				return mcp.NewToolResultError("Failed to delete memory: " + err.Error()), nil
   841			}
   842	
   843			mode := "soft-deleted"
   844			if hardDelete {
   845				mode = "permanently deleted"
   846			}
   847			return mcp.NewToolResultText(fmt.Sprintf("Memory #%d %s", id, mode)), nil
   848		}
   849	}

    10	// Feature catalog: SHA-256 content-hash deduplication
    11	// Feature catalog: Canonical ID dedup hardening
