    13	
    14	
    15	const UNCHANGED_EMBEDDING_STATUSES = new Set(['success', 'pending', 'partial']);
    16	const DEDUP_ELIGIBLE_EMBEDDING_STATUSES = ['success', 'partial'] as const;
    17	const QUALITY_SCORE_EPSILON = 1e-9;
    18	const SCOPE_COLUMNS = [
    19	  ['tenant_id', 'tenantId'],
    20	  ['user_id', 'userId'],
    21	  ['agent_id', 'agentId'],
    22	  ['session_id', 'sessionId'],
    23	  ['shared_space_id', 'sharedSpaceId'],
    24	] as const;
    25	
    26	interface SamePathDedupExclusion {
    27	  canonicalFilePath: string;
    28	  filePath: string;
    29	}
    30	
    31	import type { MemoryScopeMatch } from './types.js';
    32	import { normalizeScopeMatchValue } from './types.js';
    33	
    34	type ScopeColumnName = typeof SCOPE_COLUMNS[number][0];
    35	
    36	interface LatestMemoryLookupRow {
    37	  id: number;
    38	  content_hash: string;
    39	  embedding_status: string | null;
    40	  trigger_phrases: string | null;
    41	  quality_score: number | null;
    42	  quality_flags: string | null;
    43	}
    44	
    45	interface DuplicateLookupRow {
    46	  id: number;
    47	  file_path: string;
    48	  title: string | null;
    49	  content_text?: string | null;
    50	}
    51	
    52	function buildScopedWhereClauses(scope: MemoryScopeMatch): {
    53	  clauses: string[];
    54	  params: Array<string>;
    55	} {
    56	  const normalizedScope: MemoryScopeMatch = {
    57	    tenantId: normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.tenantId),
    58	    userId: normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.userId),
    59	    agentId: normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.agentId),
    60	    sessionId: normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.sessionId),
    61	    sharedSpaceId: normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.sharedSpaceId),
    62	  };
    63	  const clauses: string[] = [];
    64	  const params: string[] = [];
    65	
    66	  for (const [column, key] of SCOPE_COLUMNS) {
    67	    const value = normalizedScope[key];
    68	    if (value === null || value === undefined) {
    69	      clauses.push(`${column} IS NULL`);
    70	      continue;
    71	    }
    72	    clauses.push(`${column} = ?`);
    73	    params.push(value);
    74	  }
    75	
    76	  return { clauses, params };
    77	}
    78	
    79	function selectLatestExistingRow(
    80	  database: Database.Database,
    81	  parsed: ParsedMemory,
    82	  pathColumn: ScopeColumnName | 'canonical_file_path' | 'file_path',
    83	  pathValue: string,
    84	  scopeClauses: string[],
    85	  scopeParams: string[],
    86	): LatestMemoryLookupRow | undefined {
    87	  const whereClauses = [
    88	    'spec_folder = ?',
    89	    'parent_id IS NULL',
    90	    `${pathColumn} = ?`,
    91	    ...scopeClauses,
    92	  ];
    93	
    94	  return database.prepare(`
    95	    SELECT id, content_hash, embedding_status, trigger_phrases, quality_score, quality_flags
    96	    FROM memory_index
    97	    WHERE ${whereClauses.join('\n      AND ')}
    98	    ORDER BY id DESC
    99	    LIMIT 1
   100	  `).get(
   101	    parsed.specFolder,
   102	    pathValue,
   103	    ...scopeParams,
   104	  ) as LatestMemoryLookupRow | undefined;
   105	}
   106	
   107	function parseJsonStringArray(raw: string | null): string[] {
   108	  if (!raw) {
   109	    return [];
   110	  }
   111	
   112	  try {
   113	    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as unknown;
   114	    return Array.isArray(parsed)
   115	      ? parsed.filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string')
   116	      : [];
   117	  } catch {
   118	    return [];
   119	  }
   120	}
   121	
   122	function normalizeStringArray(values: string[]): string[] {
   123	  return [...new Set(values.map(value => value.trim()).filter(Boolean))].sort();
   124	}
   125	
   126	function areEquivalentStringArrays(left: string[], right: string[]): boolean {
   127	  const normalizedLeft = normalizeStringArray(left);
   128	  const normalizedRight = normalizeStringArray(right);
   129	
   130	  if (normalizedLeft.length !== normalizedRight.length) {
   131	    return false;
   132	  }
   133	
   134	  return normalizedLeft.every((value, index) => value === normalizedRight[index]);
   135	}
   136	
   137	function isSamePathMetadataEquivalent(
   138	  existing: {
   139	    trigger_phrases: string | null;
   140	    quality_score: number | null;
   141	    quality_flags: string | null;
   142	  },
   143	  parsed: ParsedMemory,
   144	): boolean {
   145	  const persistedTriggerPhrases = parseJsonStringArray(existing.trigger_phrases);
   146	  if (!areEquivalentStringArrays(persistedTriggerPhrases, parsed.triggerPhrases)) {
   147	    return false;
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  const persistedQualityFlags = parseJsonStringArray(existing.quality_flags);
   151	  if (!areEquivalentStringArrays(persistedQualityFlags, parsed.qualityFlags ?? [])) {
   152	    return false;
   153	  }
   154	
   155	  const persistedQualityScore = existing.quality_score ?? 0;
   156	  const parsedQualityScore = parsed.qualityScore ?? 0;
   157	  return Math.abs(persistedQualityScore - parsedQualityScore) <= QUALITY_SCORE_EPSILON;
   158	}
   159	
   160	function verifyStoredContentMatch(
   161	  storedContent: string | null | undefined,
   162	  storedPath: string | null | undefined,
   163	  incomingContent: string,
   164	): boolean | null {
   165	  if (typeof storedContent === 'string') {
   166	    return storedContent === incomingContent;
   167	  }
   168	
   169	  if (typeof storedPath === 'string' && storedPath.length > 0) {
   170	    try {
   171	      if (fs.existsSync(storedPath)) {
   172	        return fs.readFileSync(storedPath, 'utf-8') === incomingContent;
   173	      }
   174	    } catch {
   175	      return null;
   176	    }
   177	  }
   178	
   179	  return null;
   180	}
   181	
   182	export function checkExistingRow(
   183	  database: Database.Database,
   184	  parsed: ParsedMemory,
   185	  canonicalFilePath: string,
   186	  filePath: string,
   187	  force: boolean,
   188	  warnings: string[] | undefined,
   189	  scope: MemoryScopeMatch = {},
   190	): IndexResult | null {
   191	  const { clauses: scopeClauses, params: scopeParams } = buildScopedWhereClauses(scope);
   192	  const candidates = [
   193	    selectLatestExistingRow(
   194	      database,
   195	      parsed,
   196	      'canonical_file_path',
   197	      canonicalFilePath,
   198	      scopeClauses,
   199	      scopeParams,
   200	    ),
   201	  ];
   202	
   203	  if (filePath !== canonicalFilePath) {
   204	    candidates.push(
   205	      selectLatestExistingRow(
   206	        database,
   207	        parsed,
   208	        'file_path',
   209	        filePath,
   210	        scopeClauses,
   211	        scopeParams,
   212	      ),
   213	    );
   214	  }
   215	
   216	  const existing = candidates
   217	    .filter((candidate): candidate is LatestMemoryLookupRow => candidate !== undefined)
   218	    .sort((left, right) => right.id - left.id)[0];
   219	
   220	  const existingStatus = existing?.embedding_status ?? null;
   221	  const isUnchangedEligible = existingStatus !== null && UNCHANGED_EMBEDDING_STATUSES.has(existingStatus);
   222	  const isMetadataEquivalent = existing
   223	    ? isSamePathMetadataEquivalent(existing, parsed)
   224	    : false;
   225	
   226	  // P1-4 FIX: Check content hash even during force reindex to prevent duplicate
   227	  // row accumulation. If content AND metadata are identical, the embedding would
   228	  // not change either, so re-indexing provides no value and creates duplicates.
   229	  if (existing && existing.content_hash === parsed.contentHash && isUnchangedEligible && isMetadataEquivalent) {
   230	    return {
   231	      status: 'unchanged',
   232	      id: existing.id,
   233	      specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   234	      title: parsed.title ?? '',
   235	      triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   236	      contextType: parsed.contextType,
   237	      importanceTier: parsed.importanceTier,
   238	      warnings,
   239	    };
   240	  }
   241	
   242	  return null;
   243	}
   244	
   245	export function checkContentHashDedup(
   246	  database: Database.Database,
   247	  parsed: ParsedMemory,
   248	  force: boolean,
   249	  warnings: string[] | undefined,
   250	  samePathExclusion?: SamePathDedupExclusion,
   251	  scope: MemoryScopeMatch = {},
   252	): IndexResult | null {
   253	  if (!force) {
   254	    const { clauses: scopeClauses, params: scopeParams } = buildScopedWhereClauses(scope);
   255	    const whereClauses = [
   256	      'spec_folder = ?',
   257	      'content_hash = ?',
   258	      'parent_id IS NULL',
   259	      'embedding_status IN (?, ?)',
   260	      ...scopeClauses,
   261	    ];
   262	    const duplicateParams: Array<string> = [
   263	      parsed.specFolder,
   264	      parsed.contentHash,
   265	      ...DEDUP_ELIGIBLE_EMBEDDING_STATUSES,
   266	      ...scopeParams,
   267	    ];
   268	
   269	    if (samePathExclusion) {
   270	      whereClauses.push('file_path != ?');
   271	      duplicateParams.push(samePathExclusion.filePath);
   272	      whereClauses.push('(canonical_file_path IS NULL OR canonical_file_path != ?)');
   273	      duplicateParams.push(samePathExclusion.canonicalFilePath);
   274	    }
   275	
   276	    const duplicateByHash = database.prepare(`
   277	      SELECT id, file_path, title, content_text
   278	      FROM memory_index
   279	      WHERE ${whereClauses.join('\n        AND ')}
   280	      ORDER BY id DESC
   281	      LIMIT 1
   282	    `).get(...duplicateParams) as DuplicateLookupRow | undefined;
   283	
   284	    if (duplicateByHash) {
   285	      const verifiedMatch = verifyStoredContentMatch(
   286	        duplicateByHash.content_text,
   287	        duplicateByHash.file_path,
   288	        parsed.content,
   289	      );
   290	      if (verifiedMatch === false) {
   291	        console.warn(`[memory-save] Hash match for memory #${duplicateByHash.id} failed secondary content verification; continuing save`);
   292	        return null;
   293	      }
   294	      console.error(`[memory-save] T054: Duplicate content detected (hash match id=${duplicateByHash.id}), skipping embedding`);
   295	      return {
   296	        status: 'duplicate',
   297	        id: duplicateByHash.id,
   298	        specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   299	        title: parsed.title ?? duplicateByHash.title ?? '',
   300	        triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   301	        contextType: parsed.contextType,
   302	        importanceTier: parsed.importanceTier,
   303	        warnings,
   304	        message: `Duplicate content detected: identical to existing memory #${duplicateByHash.id} (${duplicateByHash.file_path}). Skipping embedding generation.`,
   305	      };
   306	    }
   307	  }
   308	
   309	  return null;
   310	}

   871	            status: 'error',
   872	            error: `Failed to mark predecessor ${peResult.supersededId} as superseded after chunked indexing: ${getAtomicSaveErrorMessage(supersedeErr)}`,
   873	            message: `Chunked indexing succeeded, but predecessor ${peResult.supersededId} could not be superseded.${cleanupSuffix}`,
   874	          };
   875	        }
   876	      }
   877	
   878	      if (
   879	        shouldPersistFinalizedFile
   880	        && finalizedFileContent
   881	        && (chunkedResult.status === 'indexed' || chunkedResult.status === 'updated')
   882	      ) {
   883	        try {
   884	          await finalizeMemoryFileContent(filePath, finalizedFileContent);
   885	        } catch (finalizeErr: unknown) {
   886	          finalizeWarning = `Quality-loop file persistence failed after chunked indexing: ${finalizeErr instanceof Error ? finalizeErr.message : String(finalizeErr)}`;
   887	          console.warn(`[memory-save] ${finalizeWarning}`);
   888	        }
   889	      }
   890	
   891	      if (peResult.decision.action !== 'CREATE') {
   892	        chunkedResult.pe_action = peResult.decision.action;
   893	        chunkedResult.pe_reason = peResult.decision.reason;
   894	      }
   895	      if (peResult.supersededId != null) {
   896	        chunkedResult.superseded_id = peResult.supersededId;
   897	      }
   898	      if (Array.isArray(reconResult.warnings) && reconResult.warnings.length > 0) {
   899	        const existingWarnings = Array.isArray(chunkedResult.warnings)
   900	          ? [...(chunkedResult.warnings as string[])]
   901	          : [];
   902	        chunkedResult.warnings = [...existingWarnings, ...reconResult.warnings];
   903	      }
   904	
   905	      return appendResultWarning(chunkedResult, finalizeWarning);
   906	    }
   907	
   908	    // A4 FIX: Wrap dedup-check + insert in a single transaction for DB-level
   909	    // atomicity. Uses database.transaction() so inner transaction() calls in
   910	    // createMemoryRecord / index_memory correctly nest via SAVEPOINTs instead
   911	    // of failing with "cannot start a transaction within a transaction".
   912	    // withSpecFolderLock handles in-process serialization; this transaction
   913	    // provides defense-in-depth against multi-process races.
   914	    let existing: { id: number; content_hash: string } | undefined;
   915	
   916	    // C5-1: Content-hash dedup check BEFORE the write transaction — reads are
   917	    // safe outside the transaction and this avoids an early-return inside the
   918	    // transaction callback (which would COMMIT an empty tx unnecessarily).
   919	    const dupResult = checkContentHashDedup(database, parsed, force, validation.warnings, {
   920	      canonicalFilePath,
   921	      filePath,
   922	    }, scope);
   923	    if (dupResult) {
   924	      return dupResult;
   925	    }
   926	
   927	    const writeTransaction = database.transaction((): number => {
   928	      // CREATE NEW MEMORY
   929	      existing = findSamePathExistingMemory(
   930	        database,
   931	        parsed.specFolder,
   932	        canonicalFilePath,
   933	        filePath,
   934	        scope,
   935	      ) as { id: number; content_hash: string } | undefined;
   936	
   937	      const memoryId = existing && existing.content_hash !== parsed.contentHash
   938	        ? createAppendOnlyMemoryRecord({
   939	            database,
   940	            parsed,
   941	            filePath,
   942	            embedding,
   943	            embeddingFailureReason,
   944	            predecessorMemoryId: existing.id,
   945	            actor: 'mcp:memory_save',
   946	          })
   947	        : createMemoryRecord(
   948	            database,
   949	            parsed,
   950	            filePath,
   951	            embedding,
   952	            embeddingFailureReason,
   953	            peResult.decision,
   954	            scope,
   955	          );
   956	
   957	      // F1.01 fix: Mark superseded memory AFTER new record creation, inside
   958	      // the same transaction, so a creation failure rolls back both operations.
   959	      if (peResult.supersededId != null) {
   960	        const supersededOk = markMemorySuperseded(peResult.supersededId);
   961	        // C5-3: Supersede failure should abort the transaction
   962	        if (!supersededOk) {
   963	          throw new Error(`Failed to mark predecessor ${peResult.supersededId} as superseded`);
   964	        }
   965	      }
   966	
   967	      recordLineageVersion(database, {
   968	        memoryId,
   969	        predecessorMemoryId: existing && existing.content_hash !== parsed.contentHash
   970	          ? existing.id
   971	          : null,
   972	        actor: 'mcp:memory_save',
   973	        transitionEvent: existing && existing.content_hash !== parsed.contentHash
   974	          ? 'SUPERSEDE'
   975	          : 'CREATE',
   976	      });
   977	
   978	      return memoryId;
   979	    });
   980	
   981	    const id: number = writeTransaction.immediate();
   982	
   983	    if (shouldPersistFinalizedFile && finalizedFileContent) {
   984	      try {
   985	        await finalizeMemoryFileContent(filePath, finalizedFileContent);
   986	      } catch (finalizeErr: unknown) {
   987	        finalizeWarning = `[file-persistence-failed] Quality-loop file persistence failed after DB commit: ${finalizeErr instanceof Error ? finalizeErr.message : String(finalizeErr)}. DB row committed — manual file recovery may be needed.`;
   988	        console.warn(`[memory-save] ${finalizeWarning}`);
   989	      }
   990	    }

    90	    true,

    92	  );

    94	
    95	export function findSamePathExistingMemory(
    96	  database: BetterSqlite3.Database,
    97	  specFolder: string,
    98	  canonicalFilePath: string,
    99	  filePath: string,
   100	  scope: MemoryScopeMatch = {},
   101	): { id: number; title: string | null; content_hash?: string | null } | undefined {
   102	  const tenantId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.tenantId);
   103	  const userId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.userId);
   104	  const agentId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.agentId);
   105	  const sessionId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.sessionId);
   106	  const sharedSpaceId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.sharedSpaceId);
   107	
   108	  return database.prepare(`
   109	    SELECT id, title, content_hash
   110	    FROM memory_index
   111	    WHERE spec_folder = ?
   112	      AND parent_id IS NULL
   113	      AND (canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?)
   114	      AND ((? IS NULL AND tenant_id IS NULL) OR tenant_id = ?)
   115	      AND ((? IS NULL AND user_id IS NULL) OR user_id = ?)
   116	      AND ((? IS NULL AND agent_id IS NULL) OR agent_id = ?)
   117	      AND ((? IS NULL AND session_id IS NULL) OR session_id = ?)
   118	      AND ((? IS NULL AND shared_space_id IS NULL) OR shared_space_id = ?)
   119	    ORDER BY id DESC
   120	    LIMIT 1
   121	  `).get(
   122	    specFolder,
   123	    canonicalFilePath,
   124	    filePath,
   125	    tenantId,
   126	    tenantId,
   127	    userId,
   128	    userId,
   129	    agentId,
   130	    agentId,
   131	    sessionId,
   132	    sessionId,
   133	    sharedSpaceId,
   134	    sharedSpaceId,
   135	  ) as { id: number; title: string | null; content_hash?: string | null } | undefined;
   136	}
   137	
   138	/**
   139	 * Creates a memory row with metadata, optional BM25 entry, and save history.
   140	 * Returns the persisted memory id for downstream save handlers.
   141	 */
   142	export function createMemoryRecord(
   143	  database: BetterSqlite3.Database,
   144	  parsed: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile>,
   145	  filePath: string,
   146	  embedding: Float32Array | null,
   147	  embeddingFailureReason: string | null,
   148	  peDecision: PeDecision,
   149	  scope: MemoryScopeMatch = {},
   150	): number {
   151	  if (!embedding) {
   152	    console.error(`[memory-save] Using deferred indexing for ${path.basename(filePath)}`);
   153	  }
   154	
   155	  // Detect spec level for spec documents.
   156	  const specLevel = isSpecDocumentType(parsed.documentType)
   157	    ? detectSpecLevelFromParsed(filePath)
   158	    : null;
   159	  const encodingIntent = isEncodingIntentEnabled()
   160	    ? classifyEncodingIntent(parsed.content)
   161	    : undefined;
   162	  const canonicalFilePath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
   163	  // Security: raw provider errors sanitized before persistence/response
   164	  const persistedEmbeddingFailureReason = sanitizeEmbeddingFailureMessage(embeddingFailureReason);
   165	
   166	  const indexWithMetadata = database.transaction(() => {
   167	    // Determine importance weight based on document type.
   168	    const importanceWeight = calculateDocumentWeight(filePath, parsed.documentType);
   169	    const samePathExisting = findSamePathExistingMemory(
   170	      database,
   171	      parsed.specFolder,
   172	      canonicalFilePath,
   173	      filePath,
   174	      scope,
   175	    );
   176	    const lineageRouting = resolveCreateRecordLineage(peDecision, samePathExisting?.id ?? null);
   177	    const predecessorMemoryId = lineageRouting.predecessorMemoryId;
   178	    const transitionEvent = lineageRouting.transitionEvent;
   179	
   180	    const memory_id: number = embedding
   181	      ? vectorIndex.indexMemory({
   182	          specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   183	          filePath,
   184	          title: parsed.title,
   185	          triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   186	          importanceWeight,
   187	          embedding,
   188	          encodingIntent,
   189	          documentType: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   190	          specLevel,
   191	          contentText: parsed.content,
   192	          qualityScore: parsed.qualityScore,
   193	          qualityFlags: parsed.qualityFlags,
   194	          appendOnly: predecessorMemoryId != null,
   195	        })
   196	      : vectorIndex.indexMemoryDeferred({
   197	          specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   198	          filePath,
   199	          title: parsed.title,
   200	          triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   201	          importanceWeight,
   202	          failureReason: persistedEmbeddingFailureReason,
   203	          encodingIntent,
   204	          documentType: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   205	          specLevel,
   206	          contentText: parsed.content,
   207	          qualityScore: parsed.qualityScore,
   208	          qualityFlags: parsed.qualityFlags,
   209	          appendOnly: predecessorMemoryId != null,
   210	        });
   211	
   212	    const fileMetadata = incrementalIndex.getFileMetadata(filePath);
   213	    const fileMtimeMs = fileMetadata ? fileMetadata.mtime : null;
   214	
   215	    applyPostInsertMetadata(database, memory_id, {
   216	      content_hash: parsed.contentHash,
   217	      context_type: parsed.contextType,
   218	      importance_tier: parsed.importanceTier,
   219	      memory_type: parsed.memoryType,
   220	      type_inference_source: parsed.memoryTypeSource,
   221	      stability: fsrsScheduler.DEFAULT_INITIAL_STABILITY,
   222	      difficulty: fsrsScheduler.DEFAULT_INITIAL_DIFFICULTY,
   223	      file_mtime_ms: fileMtimeMs,
   224	      encoding_intent: encodingIntent,
   225	      document_type: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   226	      spec_level: specLevel,
   227	      quality_score: parsed.qualityScore ?? 0,
   228	      quality_flags: JSON.stringify(parsed.qualityFlags ?? []),
   229	    });
   230	
   231	    if (embedding && peDecision.action === predictionErrorGate.ACTION.CREATE_LINKED && peDecision.existingMemoryId != null) {
   232	      try {
   233	        database.prepare(`
   234	          UPDATE memory_index
   235	          SET related_memories = ?
   236	          WHERE id = ?
   237	        `).run(JSON.stringify([peDecision.existingMemoryId]), memory_id);
   238	      } catch (err: unknown) {
   239	        const message = toErrorMessage(err);
   240	        console.error('[PE-Gate] Could not store related memories:', message);
   241	      }
   242	    }
   243	
   244	    if (predecessorMemoryId != null) {
   245	      database.prepare(`
   246	        UPDATE memory_index
   247	        SET importance_tier = 'deprecated',
   248	            updated_at = ?
   249	        WHERE id = ?
   250	      `).run(new Date().toISOString(), predecessorMemoryId);
   251	    }
   252	
   253	    recordLineageTransition(database, memory_id, {
   254	      actor: 'mcp:memory_save',
   255	      predecessorMemoryId,
   256	      transitionEvent,
   257	    });
   258	    recordCrossPathSupersedesEdge(
   259	      database,
   260	      memory_id,
   261	      lineageRouting.causalSupersedesMemoryId,
   262	      peDecision.reason,
   263	    );
   264	
   265	    if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   266	      try {
   267	        const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
   268	        bm25.addDocument(String(memory_id), bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText({
   269	          title: parsed.title,
   270	          content_text: parsed.content,
   271	          trigger_phrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   272	          file_path: filePath,
   273	        }));
   274	      } catch (bm25_err: unknown) {
   275	        const message = toErrorMessage(bm25_err);
   276	        console.warn(embedding
   277	          ? `[memory-save] BM25 indexing failed: ${message}`
   278	          : `[memory-save] BM25 indexing failed (deferred path): ${message}`);
   279	      }
   280	    }
   281	
   282	    // Append-first writes add a new row for every new current version.
   283	    try {
   284	      recordHistory(memory_id, 'ADD', null, parsed.title ?? filePath, 'mcp:memory_save');
   285	      if (predecessorMemoryId != null) {
   286	        recordHistory(
   287	          predecessorMemoryId,
   288	          'UPDATE',
   289	          samePathExisting?.title ?? null,
   290	          parsed.title ?? filePath,
   291	          'mcp:memory_save',
   292	        );
   293	      }
   294	    } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
   295	      // History recording is best-effort during save
   296	    }
   297	
   298	    return memory_id;
   299	  });
   300	
   301	  return indexWithMetadata();
   302	}

    68	async function handleMemoryDelete(args: DeleteArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
    69	  await checkDatabaseUpdated();
    70	
    71	  const { id, specFolder, confirm } = args;
    72	  if (!id && !specFolder) throw new Error('Either id or specFolder is required');
    73	  if (specFolder !== undefined && typeof specFolder !== 'string') throw new Error('specFolder must be a string');
    74	  if (specFolder && !id && !confirm) throw new Error('Bulk delete requires confirm: true');
    75	
    76	  let numericId: number | null = null;
    77	  if (id !== undefined && id !== null) {
    78	    numericId = parseMemoryId(id);
    79	  }
    80	
    81	  let deletedCount = 0;
    82	  let checkpointName: string | null = null;
    83	  let mutationLedgerWarning: string | null = null;
    84	  const database = vectorIndex.getDb();
    85	
    86	  if (!database) {
    87	    // Unified DB-unavailable contract for single and bulk delete paths.
    88	    return createDatabaseUnavailableDeleteResponse();
    89	  }
    90	
    91	  if (numericId !== null) {
    92	    const singleSnapshot = getMemoryHashSnapshot(database, numericId);
    93	
    94	    // T2-5 transaction wrapper — wraps single-delete path (memory delete, causal edge
    95	    // Cleanup, ledger append) in a transaction for atomicity on error.
    96	    database.transaction(() => {
    97	      deletedCount = vectorIndex.deleteMemory(numericId) ? 1 : 0;
    98	
    99	      if (deletedCount > 0) {
   100	        // Record DELETE history after confirmed delete (no FK, history rows survive).
   101	        // Placed after deleteMemory to avoid false audit rows for non-existent IDs.
   102	        try {
   103	          recordHistory(
   104	            numericId,
   105	            'DELETE',
   106	            singleSnapshot?.file_path ?? null,
   107	            null,
   108	            'mcp:memory_delete',
   109	            singleSnapshot?.spec_folder ?? null,
   110	          );
   111	        } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
   112	          // History recording is best-effort
   113	        }
   114	
   115	        causalEdges.init(database);
   116	        causalEdges.deleteEdgesForMemory(String(numericId));
   117	        // H1 FIX: Use db-specific invalidation instead of the no-op global version
   118	        clearDegreeCacheForDb(database);
   119	
   120	        const ledgerRecorded = appendMutationLedgerSafe(database, {
   121	          mutationType: 'delete',
   122	          reason: 'memory_delete: single memory delete',
   123	          priorHash: singleSnapshot?.content_hash ?? null,
   124	          newHash: mutationLedger.computeHash(`delete:${numericId}:${Date.now()}`),
   125	          linkedMemoryIds: [numericId],
   126	          decisionMeta: {
   127	            tool: 'memory_delete',
   128	            bulk: false,
   129	            memoryId: numericId,
   130	            specFolder: singleSnapshot?.spec_folder ?? null,
   131	            filePath: singleSnapshot?.file_path ?? null,
   132	          },
   133	          actor: 'mcp:memory_delete',
   134	        });
   135	        if (!ledgerRecorded) {
   136	          mutationLedgerWarning = 'Mutation ledger append failed; audit trail may be incomplete.';
   137	        }
   138	      }
   139	    })();
   140	  } else {
   141	    const memories: { id: number }[] = vectorIndex.getMemoriesByFolder(specFolder as string);
   142	    const deletedIds: number[] = [];
   143	    const hashById = new Map<number, MemoryHashSnapshot>();
   144	
   145	    if (database && 'prepare' in database) {
   146	      try {
   147	        const rows = database.prepare(`
   148	          SELECT id, content_hash, spec_folder, file_path
   149	          FROM memory_index
   150	          WHERE spec_folder = ?
   151	        `).all(specFolder as string) as MemoryHashSnapshot[];
   152	
   153	        for (const row of rows) {
   154	          hashById.set(row.id, row);
   155	        }
   156	      } catch (_err: unknown) {
   157	        // Non-fatal — bulk delete proceeds without per-memory hash snapshots; ledger entries will lack prior hashes.
   158	      }
   159	    }
   160	
   161	    if (memories.length > 0) {
   162	      const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, 19);
   163	      const proposedCheckpointName = `pre-cleanup-${timestamp}`;
   164	      try {
   165	        const checkpoint = checkpoints.createCheckpoint({
   166	          name: proposedCheckpointName,
   167	          specFolder,
   168	          metadata: {
   169	            reason: 'auto-checkpoint before bulk delete',
   170	            memoryCount: memories.length,
   171	          },
   172	        });
   173	
   174	        if (checkpoint) {
   175	          checkpointName = checkpoint.name;
   176	          console.error(`[memory-delete] Created checkpoint: ${checkpointName}`);
   177	        } else {
   178	          console.warn('[memory-delete] Checkpoint creation returned no checkpoint; proceeding without rollback.');
   179	          checkpointName = null;

   181	      } catch (cpErr: unknown) {

   183	        console.error(`[memory-delete] Failed to create checkpoint: ${message}`);
   184	        // Confirm is always true here (validated at function entry) — safe to proceed without checkpoint.
   185	        console.warn('[memory-delete] Proceeding without backup (user confirmed)');
   186	        checkpointName = null;
   187	      }
   188	    }
   189	
   190	    // Snapshot-then-delete is safe under single-process better-sqlite3; re-evaluate if multi-process support is added
   191	    causalEdges.init(database);
   192	    const bulkDeleteTx = database.transaction(() => {
   193	      for (const memory of memories) {
   194	        if (vectorIndex.deleteMemory(memory.id)) {
   195	          // Record DELETE history after confirmed delete (no FK, history rows survive).
   196	          try {

   203	              'mcp:memory_delete',
   204	              snapshot?.spec_folder ?? null,
   205	            );
   206	          } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
   207	            // History recording is best-effort inside bulk delete
   208	          }
   209	          deletedCount++;
   210	          deletedIds.push(memory.id);
   211	          causalEdges.deleteEdgesForMemory(String(memory.id));
   212	        }
   213	      }
   214	
   215	      // Mutation ledger entries written inside bulk transaction for atomicity with deletes.
   216	      for (const deletedId of deletedIds) {
   217	        const snapshot = hashById.get(deletedId) ?? null;
   218	        const ledgerRecorded = appendMutationLedgerSafe(database, {
   219	          mutationType: 'delete',
   220	          reason: 'memory_delete: bulk delete by spec folder',
   221	          priorHash: snapshot?.content_hash ?? null,
   222	          newHash: mutationLedger.computeHash(`bulk-delete:${deletedId}:${Date.now()}`),
   223	          linkedMemoryIds: [deletedId],
   224	          decisionMeta: {
   225	            tool: 'memory_delete',
   226	            bulk: true,
   227	            specFolder,
   228	            checkpoint: checkpointName,
   229	            memoryId: deletedId,
   230	            filePath: snapshot?.file_path ?? null,
   231	          },
   232	          actor: 'mcp:memory_delete',
   233	        });
   234	        if (!ledgerRecorded) {
   235	          mutationLedgerWarning = 'Mutation ledger append failed; audit trail may be incomplete.';
   236	        }
   237	      }
   238	    });
   239	    bulkDeleteTx();
   240	  }
   241	
   242	  let postMutationFeedback: ReturnType<typeof buildMutationHookFeedback> | null = null;
   243	  if (deletedCount > 0) {
   244	    let postMutationHooks: import('./mutation-hooks.js').MutationHookResult;
   245	    try {
   246	      postMutationHooks = runPostMutationHooks('delete', { specFolder, deletedCount });
   247	    } catch (hookError: unknown) {
   248	      const msg = hookError instanceof Error ? hookError.message : String(hookError);
   249	      postMutationHooks = {
   250	        latencyMs: 0, triggerCacheCleared: false,
   251	        constitutionalCacheCleared: false, toolCacheInvalidated: 0,
   252	        graphSignalsCacheCleared: false, coactivationCacheCleared: false,
   253	        errors: [msg],
   254	      };
   255	    }
   256	    postMutationFeedback = buildMutationHookFeedback('delete', postMutationHooks);
   257	  }
   258	
   259	  const summary = deletedCount > 0
   260	    ? `Deleted ${deletedCount} memory(s)`
   261	    : 'No memories found to delete';
   262	
   263	  const hints: string[] = [];
   264	  if (checkpointName) {
   265	    hints.push(`Restore with: checkpoint_restore({ name: "${checkpointName}" })`);
   266	  }
   267	  if (deletedCount === 0) {
   268	    hints.push('Use memory_list() to find existing memories');
   269	  }
   270	  if (postMutationFeedback) {
   271	    hints.push(...postMutationFeedback.hints);
   272	  }
   273	  if (mutationLedgerWarning) {
   274	    hints.push(mutationLedgerWarning);
   275	  }
   276	
   277	  const data: Record<string, unknown> = { deleted: deletedCount };
   278	  if (checkpointName) {
   279	    data.checkpoint = checkpointName;
   280	    data.restoreCommand = `checkpoint_restore({ name: "${checkpointName}" })`;
   281	  }
   282	  if (postMutationFeedback) {
   283	    data.postMutationHooks = postMutationFeedback.data;
   284	  }
   285	  if (mutationLedgerWarning) {
   286	    data.warning = mutationLedgerWarning;
   287	  }
   288	
   289	  return createMCPSuccessResponse({
   290	    tool: 'memory_delete',

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/session/session-manager.ts | sed -n '759,1060p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
   759	 * 
   760	 * Targets:
   761	 *   - working_memory: entries with last_focused older than threshold
   762	 *   - session_sent_memories: entries with sent_at older than threshold
   763	 *   - session_state: completed/interrupted sessions older than threshold
   764	 * 
   765	 * Preserves:
   766	 *   - session_learning records (permanent, never cleaned)
   767	 *   - Active sessions (session_state with status='active')
   768	 * 
   769	 * @param thresholdMs - Inactivity threshold in milliseconds (default: STALE_SESSION_THRESHOLD_MS / 24h)
   770	 */
   771	function cleanupStaleSessions(thresholdMs: number = STALE_SESSION_THRESHOLD_MS): StaleCleanupResult {
   772	  if (!db) {
   773	    return { success: false, workingMemoryDeleted: 0, sentMemoriesDeleted: 0, sessionStateDeleted: 0, errors: ['Database not initialized'] };
   774	  }
   775	
   776	  const errors: string[] = [];
   777	  let workingMemoryDeleted = 0;
   778	  let sentMemoriesDeleted = 0;
   779	  let sessionStateDeleted = 0;
   780	  const cutoffIso = new Date(Date.now() - thresholdMs).toISOString();
   781	
   782	  // 1. Clean stale working_memory entries
   783	  try {
   784	    const wmStmt = db.prepare('DELETE FROM working_memory WHERE last_focused < ?');
   785	    const wmResult = wmStmt.run(cutoffIso);
   786	    workingMemoryDeleted = wmResult.changes;
   787	  } catch (err: unknown) {
   788	    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   789	    // Table may not exist if working-memory module was never initialized
   790	    if (!msg.includes('no such table')) {
   791	      errors.push(`working_memory cleanup: ${msg}`);
   792	    }
   793	  }
   794	
   795	  // 2. Clean stale session_sent_memories entries
   796	  try {
   797	    const smStmt = db.prepare('DELETE FROM session_sent_memories WHERE sent_at < ?');
   798	    const smResult = smStmt.run(cutoffIso);
   799	    sentMemoriesDeleted = smResult.changes;
   800	  } catch (err: unknown) {
   801	    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   802	    if (!msg.includes('no such table')) {
   803	      errors.push(`session_sent_memories cleanup: ${msg}`);
   804	    }
   805	  }
   806	
   807	  // 3. Clean completed/interrupted session_state entries (NEVER clean active sessions)
   808	  try {
   809	    const ssStmt = db.prepare(
   810	      `DELETE FROM session_state WHERE status IN ('completed', 'interrupted') AND updated_at < ?`
   811	    );
   812	    const ssResult = ssStmt.run(cutoffIso);
   813	    sessionStateDeleted = ssResult.changes;
   814	  } catch (err: unknown) {
   815	    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   816	    if (!msg.includes('no such table')) {
   817	      errors.push(`session_state cleanup: ${msg}`);
   818	    }
   819	  }
   820	
   821	  const totalDeleted = workingMemoryDeleted + sentMemoriesDeleted + sessionStateDeleted;
   822	  if (totalDeleted > 0) {
   823	    console.error(
   824	      `[session-manager] Stale session cleanup: removed ${workingMemoryDeleted} working_memory, ` +
   825	      `${sentMemoriesDeleted} sent_memories, ${sessionStateDeleted} session_state entries ` +
   826	      `(threshold: ${Math.round(thresholdMs / 3600000)}h)`
   827	    );
   828	  }
   829	
   830	  if (errors.length > 0) {
   831	    console.warn(`[session-manager] Stale cleanup had ${errors.length} error(s): ${errors.join('; ')}`);
   832	  }
   833	
   834	  return {
   835	    success: errors.length === 0,
   836	    workingMemoryDeleted,
   837	    sentMemoriesDeleted,
   838	    sessionStateDeleted,
   839	    errors,
   840	  };
   841	}
   842	
   843	function clearSession(sessionId: string): CleanupResult {
   844	  if (!db || !sessionId) return { success: false, deletedCount: 0 };
   845	
   846	  try {
   847	    const stmt = db.prepare(`
   848	      DELETE FROM session_sent_memories WHERE session_id = ?
   849	    `);
   850	    const result = stmt.run(sessionId);
   851	
   852	    // Immediately clear working memory for cleared session (GAP 2).
   853	    try {
   854	      workingMemory.clearSession(sessionId);
   855	    } catch (wmErr: unknown) {
   856	      const wmMsg = wmErr instanceof Error ? wmErr.message : String(wmErr);
   857	      console.warn(`[session-manager] Working memory cleanup for ${sessionId} failed: ${wmMsg}`);
   858	    }
   859	
   860	    return { success: true, deletedCount: result.changes };
   861	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   862	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   863	    console.error(`[session-manager] clear_session failed: ${message}`);
   864	    return { success: false, deletedCount: 0 };
   865	  }
   866	}
   867	
   868	function getSessionStats(sessionId: string): SessionStats {
   869	  if (!db || !sessionId) return { totalSent: 0, oldestEntry: null, newestEntry: null };
   870	
   871	  try {
   872	    const stmt = db.prepare(`
   873	      SELECT
   874	        COUNT(*) as total_sent,
   875	        MIN(sent_at) as oldest_entry,
   876	        MAX(sent_at) as newest_entry
   877	      FROM session_sent_memories
   878	      WHERE session_id = ?
   879	    `);
   880	    const row = stmt.get(sessionId) as { total_sent: number; oldest_entry: string | null; newest_entry: string | null } | undefined;
   881	
   882	    return {
   883	      totalSent: row?.total_sent || 0,
   884	      oldestEntry: row?.oldest_entry || null,
   885	      newestEntry: row?.newest_entry || null,
   886	    };
   887	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   888	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   889	    console.warn(`[session-manager] get_session_stats failed: ${message}`);
   890	    return { totalSent: 0, oldestEntry: null, newestEntry: null };
   891	  }
   892	}
   893	
   894	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   895	   8. INTEGRATION HELPERS
   896	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   897	
   898	function filterSearchResults(sessionId: string, results: MemoryInput[]): FilterResult {
   899	  if (!SESSION_CONFIG.enabled || !sessionId || !Array.isArray(results)) {
   900	    return {
   901	      filtered: results || [],
   902	      dedupStats: { enabled: false, filtered: 0, total: results?.length || 0 },
   903	    };
   904	  }
   905	
   906	  // Reserve unsent hashes while filtering so concurrent searches cannot both inject.
   907	  const shouldSendMap = shouldSendMemoriesBatch(sessionId, results, true);
   908	  const seenBatchHashes = new Set<string>();
   909	  const filtered = results.filter((r) => {
   910	    if (r.id != null && shouldSendMap.get(r.id) === false) {
   911	      return false;
   912	    }
   913	    try {
   914	      const hash = generateMemoryHash(r);
   915	      if (seenBatchHashes.has(hash)) {
   916	        return false;
   917	      }
   918	      seenBatchHashes.add(hash);
   919	    } catch {
   920	      // If hash generation fails unexpectedly, preserve existing behavior.
   921	      return true;
   922	    }
   923	    return true;
   924	  });
   925	  const filteredCount = results.length - filtered.length;
   926	
   927	  return {
   928	    filtered,
   929	    dedupStats: {
   930	      enabled: true,
   931	      filtered: filteredCount,
   932	      total: results.length,
   933	      tokenSavingsEstimate: filteredCount > 0 ? `~${filteredCount * 200} tokens` : '0',
   934	    },
   935	  };
   936	}
   937	
   938	function markResultsSent(sessionId: string, results: MemoryInput[]): MarkBatchResult {
   939	  if (!SESSION_CONFIG.enabled || !sessionId || !Array.isArray(results) || results.length === 0) {
   940	    return { success: true, markedCount: 0 };
   941	  }
   942	
   943	  return markMemoriesSentBatch(sessionId, results);
   944	}
   945	
   946	function isEnabled(): boolean {
   947	  return SESSION_CONFIG.enabled;
   948	}
   949	
   950	function getConfig(): SessionConfig {
   951	  return { ...SESSION_CONFIG };
   952	}
   953	
   954	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   955	   9. SESSION STATE MANAGEMENT
   956	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   957	
   958	const SESSION_STATE_SCHEMA_SQL = `
   959	  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS session_state (
   960	    session_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   961	    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK(status IN ('active', 'completed', 'interrupted')),
   962	    spec_folder TEXT,
   963	    current_task TEXT,
   964	    last_action TEXT,
   965	    context_summary TEXT,
   966	    pending_work TEXT,
   967	    state_data TEXT,
   968	    created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
   969	    updated_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
   970	  );
   971	`;
   972	
   973	const SESSION_STATE_INDEX_SQL: string[] = [
   974	  'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_session_state_status ON session_state(status);',
   975	  'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_session_state_updated ON session_state(updated_at);',
   976	  'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_session_state_identity_scope ON session_state(tenant_id, user_id, agent_id);',
   977	];
   978	
   979	const SESSION_STATE_MIGRATIONS: Array<{ column: string; sql: string }> = [
   980	  { column: 'tenant_id', sql: 'ALTER TABLE session_state ADD COLUMN tenant_id TEXT;' },
   981	  { column: 'user_id', sql: 'ALTER TABLE session_state ADD COLUMN user_id TEXT;' },
   982	  { column: 'agent_id', sql: 'ALTER TABLE session_state ADD COLUMN agent_id TEXT;' },
   983	];
   984	
   985	function getTableColumns(tableName: string): Set<string> {
   986	  if (!db) {
   987	    return new Set();
   988	  }
   989	
   990	  const rows = db.prepare(`PRAGMA table_info(${tableName})`).all() as Array<{ name?: string }>;
   991	  return new Set(
   992	    rows
   993	      .map((row) => row.name)
   994	      .filter((name): name is string => typeof name === 'string' && name.length > 0),
   995	  );
   996	}
   997	
   998	function ensureSessionStateSchema(): InitResult {
   999	  if (!db) return { success: false, error: 'Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up.' };
  1000	
  1001	  try {
  1002	    db.exec(SESSION_STATE_SCHEMA_SQL);
  1003	    const existingColumns = getTableColumns('session_state');
  1004	    for (const migration of SESSION_STATE_MIGRATIONS) {
  1005	      if (!existingColumns.has(migration.column)) {
  1006	        db.exec(migration.sql);
  1007	      }
  1008	    }
  1009	    for (const indexSql of SESSION_STATE_INDEX_SQL) {
  1010	      db.exec(indexSql);
  1011	    }
  1012	    return { success: true };
  1013	  } catch (error: unknown) {
  1014	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
  1015	    console.error(`[session-manager] Session state schema creation failed: ${message}`);
  1016	    return { success: false, error: message };
  1017	  }
  1018	}
  1019	
  1020	function saveSessionState(sessionId: string, state: SessionStateInput = {}): InitResult {
  1021	  if (!db) return { success: false, error: 'Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up.' };
  1022	  if (!sessionId || typeof sessionId !== 'string') {
  1023	    return { success: false, error: 'Valid sessionId is required' };
  1024	  }
  1025	
  1026	  try {
  1027	    ensureSessionStateSchema();
  1028	    const now = new Date().toISOString();

  1030	    const tenantId = normalizeIdentityValue(state.tenantId);
  1031	    const userId = normalizeIdentityValue(state.userId);
  1032	    const agentId = normalizeIdentityValue(state.agentId);
  1033	
  1034	    const stmt = db.prepare(`
  1035	      INSERT INTO session_state (
  1036	        session_id, status, spec_folder, current_task, last_action,
  1037	        context_summary, pending_work, state_data,
  1038	        tenant_id, user_id, agent_id,
  1039	        created_at, updated_at
  1040	      )
  1041	      VALUES (?, 'active', ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
  1042	      ON CONFLICT(session_id) DO UPDATE SET
  1043	        status = 'active',
  1044	        spec_folder = COALESCE(excluded.spec_folder, session_state.spec_folder),
  1045	        current_task = COALESCE(excluded.current_task, session_state.current_task),
  1046	        last_action = COALESCE(excluded.last_action, session_state.last_action),
  1047	        context_summary = COALESCE(excluded.context_summary, session_state.context_summary),
  1048	        pending_work = COALESCE(excluded.pending_work, session_state.pending_work),
  1049	        state_data = COALESCE(excluded.state_data, session_state.state_data),
  1050	        tenant_id = COALESCE(excluded.tenant_id, session_state.tenant_id),
  1051	        user_id = COALESCE(excluded.user_id, session_state.user_id),
  1052	        agent_id = COALESCE(excluded.agent_id, session_state.agent_id),
  1053	        updated_at = excluded.updated_at
  1054	    `);
  1055	
  1056	    stmt.run(
  1057	      sessionId,
  1058	      state.specFolder || null,
  1059	      state.currentTask || null,
  1060	      state.lastAction || null,

   204	  return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : null;
   205	}
   206	
   207	function buildScopePrefix(row: MemoryIndexRow): string | null {
   208	  const scopeTuple = [

   210	    ['user', normalizeScopeValue(row.user_id)],
   211	    ['agent', normalizeScopeValue(row.agent_id)],
   212	    ['session', normalizeScopeValue(row.session_id)],
   213	    ['shared_space', normalizeScopeValue(row.shared_space_id)],
   214	  ].filter((entry): entry is [string, string] => entry[1] != null);

   216	  if (scopeTuple.length === 0) {
   217	    return null;
   218	  }
   219

   221	    .update(JSON.stringify(scopeTuple), 'utf8')
   222	    .digest('hex')
   223	    .slice(0, 24);
   224	
   225	  return `scope-sha256:${scopeHash}`;
   226	}

     7	// DECAY STRATEGY (ADR-004): Search-time temporal decay uses an
     8	// FSRS-preferred strategy. Memories with FSRS review data (last_review
     9	// IS NOT NULL, review_count > 0) use the FSRS v4 power-law formula:
    10	// R(t) = (1 + 0.2346 * t / S)^(-0.5)

   116	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   117	   1. CONFIGURATION — Embedding Dimension
   118	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   119	
   120	/** Default embedding dimension used by the vector index. */
   121	export const EMBEDDING_DIM = 768;
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * Gets the active embedding dimension for the current provider.
   125	 * @returns The embedding dimension.
   126	 */
   127	export function get_embedding_dim(): number {
   128	  return getStartupEmbeddingDimension();
   129	}
   130	
   131	/**
   132	 * Waits for the embedding provider to report a stable dimension.
   133	 * @param timeout_ms - The maximum time to wait in milliseconds.
   134	 * @returns A promise that resolves to the confirmed embedding dimension.
   135	 */
   136	export async function get_confirmed_embedding_dimension(timeout_ms = 5000): Promise<number> {
   137	  const start = Date.now();
   138	  while (Date.now() - start < timeout_ms) {
   139	    const dim = get_embedding_dim();
   140	    if (dim !== 768 || process.env.EMBEDDING_DIM === '768') {
   141	      return dim;
   142	    }
   143	    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
   144	  }
   145	  console.warn('[vector-index] Using default dimension 768 after timeout');
   146	  return 768;
   147	}
   148	
   149	type EmbeddingDimensionValidation = {
   150	  valid: boolean;
   151	  stored: number | null;
   152	  current: number | null;
   153	  reason?: string;
   154	  warning?: string;
   155	};
   156	
   157	type StoredEmbeddingDimension = {
   158	  existing_db: boolean;
   159	  stored_dim: number | null;
   160	  source: 'vec_metadata' | 'vec_memories' | null;
   161	  reason?: string;
   162	};
   163	
   164	function get_existing_embedding_dimension(
   165	  database: Database.Database,
   166	): StoredEmbeddingDimension {
   167	  const schema_row = database.prepare(`
   168	    SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_index'
   169	  `).get();
   170	
   171	  if (!schema_row) {
   172	    return {
   173	      existing_db: false,
   174	      stored_dim: null,
   175	      source: null,
   176	      reason: 'No existing schema',
   177	    };
   178	  }
   179	
   180	  const metadata_table = database.prepare(`
   181	    SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='vec_metadata'
   182	  `).get();
   183	
   184	  if (metadata_table) {
   185	    const stored_row = database.prepare(`
   186	      SELECT value FROM vec_metadata WHERE key = 'embedding_dim'
   187	    `).get() as { value: string } | undefined;
   188	
   189	    if (stored_row) {
   190	      const stored_dim = parseInt(stored_row.value, 10);
   191	      if (Number.isFinite(stored_dim) && stored_dim > 0) {
   192	        return {
   193	          existing_db: true,
   194	          stored_dim,
   195	          source: 'vec_metadata',
   196	        };
   197	      }
   198	    }
   199	  }
   200	
   201	  const vec_table = database.prepare(`
   202	    SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE name='vec_memories'
   203	  `).get() as { sql: string | null } | undefined;
   204	  const vec_sql = typeof vec_table?.sql === 'string' ? vec_table.sql : null;
   205	  const match = vec_sql?.match(/FLOAT\[(\d+)\]/i);
   206	
   207	  if (match) {
   208	    const stored_dim = parseInt(match[1], 10);
   209	    if (Number.isFinite(stored_dim) && stored_dim > 0) {
   210	      return {
   211	        existing_db: true,
   212	        stored_dim,
   213	        source: 'vec_memories',
   214	      };
   215	    }
   216	  }
   217	
   218	  return {
   219	    existing_db: true,
   220	    stored_dim: null,
   221	    source: null,
   222	    reason: 'No stored vector dimension found for existing schema',
   223	  };
   224	}
   225	
   226	function validate_embedding_dimension_for_connection(
   227	  database: Database.Database | null,
   228	  _sqlite_vec_available: boolean,
   229	): EmbeddingDimensionValidation {
   230	  if (!database) {
   231	    return { valid: true, stored: null, current: null, reason: 'No database' };
   232	  }
   233	
   234	  try {
   235	    const current_dim = get_embedding_dim();
   236	    const existing = get_existing_embedding_dimension(database);
   237	
   238	    if (!existing.existing_db) {
   239	      return { valid: true, stored: null, current: current_dim, reason: existing.reason };
   240	    }
   241	
   242	    if (existing.stored_dim == null) {
   243	      return { valid: true, stored: null, current: current_dim, reason: existing.reason || 'No stored dimension' };
   244	    }
   245	
   246	    if (existing.stored_dim !== current_dim) {
   247	      const source_label = existing.source === 'vec_metadata' ? 'vec_metadata' : 'vec_memories schema';
   248	      const warning = `EMBEDDING DIMENSION MISMATCH: Existing database stores ${existing.stored_dim}-dim vectors (${source_label}), ` +
   249	        `but the active embedding configuration resolves to ${current_dim}. Refusing to bootstrap because vector search would fail. ` +
   250	        `Solutions: 1) Delete database and re-index, 2) Set EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER to match original, ` +
   251	        `3) Use MEMORY_DB_PATH for provider-specific databases.`;
   252	      console.error(`[vector-index] WARNING: ${warning}`);
   253	      return { valid: false, stored: existing.stored_dim, current: current_dim, warning };
   254	    }
   255	
   256	    return { valid: true, stored: existing.stored_dim, current: current_dim };
   257	  } catch (e: unknown) {
   258	    console.warn('[vector-index] Dimension validation error:', get_error_message(e));
   259	    return { valid: true, stored: null, current: get_embedding_dim(), reason: get_error_message(e) };
   260	  }

I’ve got the comparison scaffold now. Engram’s model is centering on single-row topic threads plus counters and tombstones, while Public is centering on append-first lineage, content-hash dedup, and session/result cleanup rather than in-row revision counters. I’m reading the remaining Public lineage and archive mechanics so the recommendation labels stay precise.

   666	export function recordLineageTransition(
   667	  database: Database.Database,
   668	  memoryId: number,
   669	  options: RecordLineageTransitionOptions = {},
   670	): RecordedLineageTransition {
   671	  bindHistory(database);
   672	  ensureLineageTables(database);
   673	
   674	  const cached = getExistingLineageTransition(database, memoryId);
   675	  if (cached) return cached;
   676	
   677	  for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= MAX_LINEAGE_VERSION_RETRIES; attempt += 1) {
   678	    // A1/B14: Wrap predecessor UPDATE + lineage INSERT + projection UPSERT in a transaction.
   679	    const recordTransitionTx = database.transaction(() => {
   680	      const row = getMemoryRow(database, memoryId);
   681	      const rowLogicalKey = buildLogicalKey(row);
   682	      const actor = options.actor ?? 'system';
   683	      const transitionEvent = options.transitionEvent ?? 'CREATE';

   685	      const predecessorMemoryId = options.predecessorMemoryId ?? null;
   686	      const validFrom = options.validFrom ?? normalizeTimestamp(row.updated_at ?? row.created_at);

   689	      let rootMemoryId = memoryId;
   690	      let versionNumber = 1;
   691	      let predecessor: LineageRow | null = null;
   692	
   693	      if (predecessorMemoryId != null) {
   694	        predecessor = getLineageRow(database, predecessorMemoryId);
   695	        if (predecessor) {
   696	          if (predecessor.logical_key !== rowLogicalKey) {
   697	            throw new Error(
   698	              `E_LINEAGE: predecessor ${predecessorMemoryId} logical identity ${predecessor.logical_key} ` +
   699	              `does not match memory ${memoryId} logical identity ${rowLogicalKey}`,
   700	            );
   701	          }
   702	          logicalKey = predecessor.logical_key;
   703	          rootMemoryId = predecessor.root_memory_id;
   704	          versionNumber = predecessor.version_number + 1;
   705	        } else {
   706	          const seeded = seedLineageFromCurrentState(database, predecessorMemoryId, {
   707	            actor,
   708	            transitionEvent: 'BACKFILL',
   709	          });

   712	              `E_LINEAGE: predecessor ${predecessorMemoryId} logical identity ${seeded.logicalKey} ` +
   713	              `does not match memory ${memoryId} logical identity ${rowLogicalKey}`,
   714	            );
   715	          }
   716	          logicalKey = seeded.logicalKey;
   717	          rootMemoryId = seeded.rootMemoryId;

   720	        }

   737	            'COALESCE will preserve the existing valid_to.',

   740	        database.prepare(`

   743	              superseded_by_memory_id = COALESCE(superseded_by_memory_id, ?)

  1250	/**
  1251	 * Compatibility wrapper used by roadmap tests and save flows for lineage writes.

  1253	 * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.

  1257	export function recordLineageVersion(

   138		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /sync/status", s.handleSyncStatus)
   139	}
   140	
   400		limit := queryInt(r, "limit", 20)
   401	
   402		prompts, err := s.store.RecentPrompts(project, limit)
   403		if err != nil {
   404			jsonError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, err.Error())
   405			return
   406		}
   407	
   408		jsonResponse(w, http.StatusOK, prompts)
   409	}
   410	
   411	func (s *Server) handleSearchPrompts(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   412		query := r.URL.Query().Get("q")
   413		if query == "" {
   414			jsonError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "q parameter is required")
   415			return
   416		}
   417	
   418		prompts, err := s.store.SearchPrompts(
   419			query,
   420			r.URL.Query().Get("project"),
   421			queryInt(r, "limit", 10),
   422		)
   423		if err != nil {
   424			jsonError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, err.Error())
   425			return
   426		}
   427	
   428		jsonResponse(w, http.StatusOK, prompts)
   429	}
   430

   505	function syncVectorOnArchive(memoryId: number): void {

   718	// Valid statuses: 'pending', 'success', 'failed', 'retry', 'partial'
   719	/**
   720	 * Updates the embedding status for a memory.
   721	 * @param id - The memory identifier.
   722	 * @param status - The new embedding status.
   723	 * @returns True when the status was updated.
   724	 */
   725	export function update_embedding_status(
   726	  id: number,
   727	  status: string,
   728	  database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
   729	): boolean {
   730	  const valid_statuses = ['pending', 'success', 'failed', 'retry', 'partial'];
   731	  if (!valid_statuses.includes(status)) {
   732	    console.warn(`[vector-index] Invalid embedding status: ${status}`);
   733	    return false;
   734	  }
   735	
   736	  try {
   737	    const result = database.prepare(`
   738	      UPDATE memory_index
   739	      SET embedding_status = ?, updated_at = datetime('now')
   740	      WHERE id = ?
   741	    `).run(status, id);
   742	
   743	    return result.changes > 0;
   744	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   745	    console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to update embedding status for ${id}: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
   746	    return false;
   747	  }
   748	}
   749	
   750	/**
   751	 * Updates the confidence value for a memory.
   752	 * @param memory_id - The memory identifier.
   753	 * @param confidence - The confidence value to store.
   754	 * @returns True when the confidence was updated.
   755	 */
   756	export function update_confidence(
   757	  memory_id: number,
   758	  confidence: number,
   759	  database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
   760	): boolean {
   761	  if (typeof confidence !== 'number' || confidence < 0 || confidence > 1) {
   762	    console.warn(`[vector-index] Invalid confidence value: ${confidence}`);
   763	    return false;
   764	  }
   765	
   766	  try {
   767	    const result = database.prepare(`
   768	      UPDATE memory_index
   769	      SET confidence = ?
   770	      WHERE id = ?
   771	    `).run(confidence, memory_id);
   772	
   773	    return result.changes > 0;
   774	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   775	    console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to update confidence for ${memory_id}: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
   776	    return false;
   777	  }
   778	}

   561	/**
   562	 * Deletes the latest memory for a file path and optional anchor.
   563	 * @param spec_folder - The owning spec folder.
   564	 * @param file_path - The file path to delete.
   565	 * @param anchor_id - The optional anchor identifier.
   566	 * @returns True when a memory was deleted.
   567	 * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates delete failures from the underlying mutation helpers.
   568	 * @example
   569	 * ```ts
   570	 * const deleted = delete_memory_by_path('specs/001-demo', 'spec.md');
   571	 * ```
   572	 */
   573	export function delete_memory_by_path(
   574	  spec_folder: string,
   575	  file_path: string,
   576	  anchor_id: string | null = null,
   577	  database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
   578	): boolean {
   579	  const canonicalPath = getCanonicalPathKey(file_path);
   580	
   581	  const row = database.prepare(`
   582	    SELECT id, spec_folder FROM memory_index
   583	    WHERE spec_folder = ?
   584	      AND (canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?)
   585	      AND (anchor_id = ? OR (anchor_id IS NULL AND ? IS NULL))
   586	    ORDER BY id DESC
   587	    LIMIT 1
   588	  `).get(spec_folder, canonicalPath, file_path, anchor_id, anchor_id) as { id: number; spec_folder?: string | null } | undefined;
   589	
   590	  if (row) {
   591	    const deleted = delete_memory_from_database(database, row.id);
   592	    if (deleted) {
   593	      // Self-record DELETE history only after the delete succeeded.
   594	      try {
   595	        recordHistory(row.id, 'DELETE', file_path ?? null, null, 'mcp:delete_by_path', row.spec_folder ?? spec_folder);
   596	      } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
   597	        // BEST-EFFORT HISTORY WRITES MUST NOT MASK A SUCCESSFUL DELETE.
   598	      }
   599	    }
   600	    return deleted;
   601	  }
   602	  return false;
   603	}
   604	
   605	/**
   606	 * Deletes multiple memories in a single transaction.
   607	 * @param memory_ids - The memory identifiers to delete.
   608	 * @returns Counts for deleted and failed items.
   609	 * @throws {VectorIndexError} When one or more deletes fail and the transaction is rolled back.
   610	 * @example
   611	 * ```ts
   612	 * const outcome = delete_memories([1, 2, 3]);
   613	 * ```
   614	 */
   615	export function delete_memories(
   616	  memory_ids: number[],
   617	  database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
   618	): { deleted: number; failed: number } {
   619	  if (!memory_ids || memory_ids.length === 0) {
   620	    return { deleted: 0, failed: 0 };
   621	  }
   622	  const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
   623	  let deleted = 0;
   624	  let failed = 0;
   625	  const deletedIds: number[] = [];
   626	
   627	  const specFolderById = new Map<number, string | null>();
   628	  for (const id of memory_ids) {
   629	    if (!Number.isInteger(id) || id <= 0) {
   630	      continue;
   631	    }
   632	    try {
   633	      const row = database.prepare('SELECT spec_folder FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(id) as { spec_folder?: string | null } | undefined;
   634	      specFolderById.set(id, row?.spec_folder ?? null);
   635	    } catch (_error: unknown) {
   636	      specFolderById.set(id, null);
   637	    }
   638	  }
   639	
   640	  const delete_transaction = database.transaction(() => {
   641	    let transactionDeleted = 0;
   642	    let transactionFailed = 0;
   643	    const failed_ids: number[] = [];
   644	
   645	    for (const id of memory_ids) {
   646	      try {
   647	        if (sqlite_vec) {
   648	          try {
   649	            database.prepare('DELETE FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid = ?').run(BigInt(id));
   650	          } catch (vec_error: unknown) {
   651	            console.warn(`[VectorIndex] Failed to delete vector for memory ${id}: ${get_error_message(vec_error)}`);
   652	          }
   653	        }
   654	
   655	        deleteAncillaryMemoryRows(database, id);
   656	        const result = database.prepare('DELETE FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').run(id);
   657	        if (result.changes > 0) {
   658	          // Memory_history rows are intentionally preserved after deletion
   659	          // So DELETE audit events recorded here persist as audit trail.
   660	          try {
   661	            recordHistory(id, 'DELETE', null, null, 'mcp:delete_memories', specFolderById.get(id) ?? null);
   662	          } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
   663	            // BEST-EFFORT HISTORY WRITES MUST NOT MASK A SUCCESSFUL DELETE.
   664	          }
   665	          transactionDeleted++;
   666	          deletedIds.push(id);
   667	        } else {
   668	          transactionFailed++;
   669	          failed_ids.push(id);
   670	        }
   671	      } catch (e: unknown) {
   672	        console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to delete memory ${id}: ${get_error_message(e)}`);
   673	        transactionFailed++;
   674	        failed_ids.push(id);
   675	      }
   676	    }
   677	
   678	    if (failed_ids.length > 0) {
   679	      throw new VectorIndexError(
   680	        `Failed to delete memories: ${failed_ids.join(', ')}. Transaction rolled back.`,
   681	        VectorIndexErrorCode.MUTATION_FAILED,
   682	      );
   683	    }
   684	
   685	    return {
   686	      deleted: transactionDeleted,
   687	      failed: transactionFailed,
   688	    };
   689	  });

   691	  try {
   692	    const outcome = delete_transaction();
   693	    deleted = outcome.deleted;
   694	    failed = outcome.failed;
   695	    if (deleted > 0) {
   696	      clear_constitutional_cache();

   700	          const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();

   501	export function delete_memory(id: number, database: Database.Database = initialize_db()): boolean {

   504	
   505	/**
   506	 * Deletes a memory and related index records using the provided database handle.
   507	 * @param database - The database containing the target memory.
   508	 * @param id - The memory identifier.
   509	 * @returns True when a memory was deleted.
   510	 * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates mutation failures encountered while deleting the memory.
   511	 * @example
   512	 * ```ts
   513	 * const deleted = delete_memory_from_database(database, 42);
   514	 * ```
   515	 */
   516	export function delete_memory_from_database(database: Database.Database, id: number): boolean {
   517	  const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
   518	
   519	  const delete_memory_tx = database.transaction(() => {
   520	    // Memory_history rows are intentionally preserved after deletion
   521	    // So DELETE audit events recorded by handlers persist as audit trail.
   522	
   523	    if (sqlite_vec) {
   524	      try {
   525	        database.prepare('DELETE FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid = ?').run(BigInt(id));
   526	      } catch (e: unknown) {
   527	        if (!isExpectedMissingVecMemoriesTable(e)) {
   528	          throw new VectorIndexError(
   529	            `Vector deletion failed for memory ${id}: ${get_error_message(e)}`,
   530	            VectorIndexErrorCode.MUTATION_FAILED,
   531	          );
   532	        }
   533	      }
   534	    }
   535	
   536	    // BUG-020: Clean ancillary records so deletes do not leave graph residue behind.
   537	    deleteAncillaryMemoryRows(database, id);
   538	
   539	    const result = database.prepare('DELETE FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').run(id);
   540	
   541	    clear_search_cache();
   542	    clear_constitutional_cache();
   543	
   544	    return result.changes > 0;
   545	  });
