     4	// Feature catalog: Hybrid search pipeline
     5	// Split from vector-index-store.ts — contains ALL mutation functions:
     6	// Index, update, delete, and status/confidence updates.
     7	
     8	import * as embeddingsProvider from '../providers/embeddings.js';
     9	import { clearGraphSignalsCache } from '../graph/graph-signals.js';
    10	import { recordHistory } from '../storage/history.js';
    11	import { getCanonicalPathKey } from '../utils/canonical-path.js';
    12	import { createLogger } from '../utils/logger.js';
    13	import { clearDegreeCacheForDb } from './graph-search-fn.js';
    14	import * as bm25Index from './bm25-index.js';
    15	import {
    16	  clear_search_cache,
    17	} from './vector-index-aliases.js';
    18	import {
    19	  get_error_message,
    20	  to_embedding_buffer,
    21	  VectorIndexError,
    22	  VectorIndexErrorCode,
    23	} from './vector-index-types.js';
    24	import {
    25	  clear_constitutional_cache,
    26	  get_embedding_dim,
    27	  initialize_db,
    28	  init_prepared_statements,
    29	  refresh_interference_scores_for_folder,
    30	  sqlite_vec_available as get_sqlite_vec_available,
    31	} from './vector-index-store.js';
    32	import type {
    33	  IndexMemoryParams as SharedIndexMemoryParams,
    34	  UpdateMemoryParams as SharedUpdateMemoryParams,
    35	} from './vector-index-types.js';
    36	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
    37	
    38	const logger = createLogger('VectorIndex');
    39	
    40	function isExpectedMissingVecMemoriesTable(error: unknown): boolean {
    41	  const message = get_error_message(error).toLowerCase();
    42	  return message.includes('no such table') && message.includes('vec_memories');
    43	}
    44	
    45	function invalidateGraphCaches(database: Database.Database): void {
    46	  try {
    47	    clearDegreeCacheForDb(database);
    48	  } catch (_error: unknown) {
    49	    // Degree cache invalidation is best-effort for legacy mutation paths.
    50	  }
    51	
    52	  try {
    53	    clearGraphSignalsCache();
    54	  } catch (_error: unknown) {
    55	    // Graph signal cache invalidation is best-effort for legacy mutation paths.
    56	  }
    57	}
    58	
    59	function deleteAncillaryMemoryRows(database: Database.Database, id: number): void {
    60	  const ancillaryTables = [
    61	    'DELETE FROM degree_snapshots WHERE memory_id = ?',
    62	    'DELETE FROM community_assignments WHERE memory_id = ?',
    63	    'DELETE FROM memory_summaries WHERE memory_id = ?',
    64	    'DELETE FROM memory_entities WHERE memory_id = ?',
    65	    'DELETE FROM memory_lineage WHERE memory_id = ?',
    66	    'DELETE FROM shared_space_conflicts WHERE existing_memory_id = ? OR incoming_memory_id = ?',
    67	  ];
    68	
    69	  for (const sql of ancillaryTables) {
    70	    try {
    71	      const paramCount = (sql.match(/\?/g) || []).length;
    72	      if (paramCount === 2) {
    73	        database.prepare(sql).run(id, id);
    74	      } else {
    75	        database.prepare(sql).run(id);
    76	      }
    77	    } catch (_error: unknown) {
    78	      // Best-effort for legacy databases that may not have these tables yet.
    79	    }
    80	  }
    81	
    82	  // B10: Clean active_memory_projection rows referencing this memory.
    83	  try {
    84	    database.prepare('DELETE FROM active_memory_projection WHERE active_memory_id = ?').run(id);
    85	  } catch (_error: unknown) {
    86	    // Best-effort for legacy databases that may not have projection tables yet.
    87	  }
    88	
    89	  try {
    90	    const memoryIdText = String(id);
    91	    database.prepare(`
    92	      DELETE FROM causal_edges
    93	      WHERE source_id IN (?, ?)
    94	         OR target_id IN (?, ?)
    95	    `).run(id, memoryIdText, id, memoryIdText);
    96	    invalidateGraphCaches(database);
    97	  } catch (_error: unknown) {
    98	    // Best-effort for legacy databases that may not have causal edges yet.
    99	  }
   100	}
   101	
   102	function upsert_active_projection(
   103	  database: ReturnType<typeof initialize_db>,
   104	  specFolder: string,
   105	  canonicalFilePath: string,
   106	  anchorId: string | null,
   107	  memoryId: number,
   108	  updatedAt: string,
   109	): void {
   110	  const logicalKey = `${specFolder}::${canonicalFilePath}::${anchorId && anchorId.trim().length > 0 ? anchorId : '_'}`;
   111	  // Evict any stale projection row that maps a *different* logical_key to the
   112	  // same active_memory_id — prevents UNIQUE constraint violation on re-index
   113	  // when the logical_key changes (e.g. anchor or path normalization drift).
   114	  database.prepare(
   115	    'DELETE FROM active_memory_projection WHERE active_memory_id = ? AND logical_key != ?',
   116	  ).run(memoryId, logicalKey);
   117	  database.prepare(`
   118	    INSERT INTO active_memory_projection (logical_key, root_memory_id, active_memory_id, updated_at)
   119	    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
   120	    ON CONFLICT(logical_key) DO UPDATE SET
   121	      root_memory_id = excluded.root_memory_id,
   122	      active_memory_id = excluded.active_memory_id,
   123	      updated_at = excluded.updated_at
   124	  `).run(logicalKey, memoryId, memoryId, updatedAt);
   125	}
   126	
   127	// These mutation-local extensions are intentional until the shared query/store
   128	// contract adopts appendOnly and canonicalFilePath semantics.
   129	type IndexMemoryParams = Readonly<SharedIndexMemoryParams> & {
   130	  readonly appendOnly?: boolean;
   131	};
   132	type IndexMemoryDeferredParams = Omit<IndexMemoryParams, 'embedding'> & {
   133	  readonly failureReason?: string | null;
   134	};
   135	type UpdateMemoryParams = Readonly<SharedUpdateMemoryParams> & {
   136	  readonly canonicalFilePath?: string;
   137	};
   138	
   139	/**
   140	 * Indexes a memory with an embedding payload.
   141	 * @param params - The memory values to index.
   142	 * @returns The indexed memory identifier.
   143	 * @throws {VectorIndexError} When embedding validation fails or the mutation cannot be applied.
   144	 * @example
   145	 * ```ts
   146	 * const id = index_memory({
   147	 *   specFolder: 'specs/001-demo',
   148	 *   filePath: 'spec.md',
   149	 *   embedding,
   150	 * });
   151	 * ```
   152	 */
   153	export function index_memory(
   154	  params: IndexMemoryParams,
   155	  database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
   156	): number {
   157	  const {
   158	    specFolder,
   159	    filePath,
   160	    parentId = null,
   161	    anchorId = null,
   162	    title = null,
   163	    triggerPhrases = [],
   164	    importanceWeight = 0.5,
   165	    embedding,
   166	    encodingIntent,
   167	    documentType = 'memory',
   168	    specLevel = null,
   169	    contentText = null,
   170	    qualityScore = 0,
   171	    qualityFlags = [],
   172	    appendOnly = false,
   173	  } = params;
   174	
   175	  if (!embedding) {
   176	    throw new VectorIndexError('Embedding is required', VectorIndexErrorCode.EMBEDDING_VALIDATION);
   177	  }
   178	
   179	  const expected_dim = get_embedding_dim();
   180	  if (embedding.length !== expected_dim) {
   180	  if (embedding.length !== expected_dim) {
   181	    console.warn(`[vector-index] Embedding dimension mismatch: expected ${expected_dim}, got ${embedding.length}`);
   182	    throw new VectorIndexError(
   183	      `Embedding must be ${expected_dim} dimensions, got ${embedding.length}`,
   184	      VectorIndexErrorCode.EMBEDDING_VALIDATION,
   185	    );
   186	  }
   187	
   188	  const now = new Date().toISOString();
   189	  const triggers_json = JSON.stringify(triggerPhrases);
   190	  const embedding_buffer = to_embedding_buffer(embedding);
   191	  const canonicalFilePath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
   192	
   193	  const stmts = init_prepared_statements(database);
   194	  const existing = appendOnly
   195	    ? null
   196	    : stmts.get_by_folder_and_path.get(specFolder, canonicalFilePath, filePath, anchorId, anchorId);
   197	
   198	  if (existing && !appendOnly) {
   199	    return update_memory({
   200	      id: existing.id,
   201	      title: title ?? undefined,
   202	      triggerPhrases,
   203	      importanceWeight,
   204	      embedding,
   205	      encodingIntent,
   206	      documentType,
   207	      specLevel,
   208	      contentText,
   209	      qualityScore,
   210	      qualityFlags,
   211	      canonicalFilePath,
   212	    }, database);
   213	  }
   214	
   215	  const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
   216	
   217	  const index_memory_tx = database.transaction(() => {
   218	    const embedding_status = sqlite_vec ? 'success' : 'pending';
   219	
   220	    const result = database.prepare(`
   221	      INSERT INTO memory_index (
   222	        spec_folder, file_path, canonical_file_path, anchor_id, title, trigger_phrases,
   223	        importance_weight, created_at, updated_at, embedding_model,
   224	        embedding_generated_at, embedding_status, encoding_intent, document_type, spec_level,
   225	        content_text, quality_score, quality_flags, parent_id
   226	      ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   227	    `).run(
   228	      specFolder, filePath, canonicalFilePath, anchorId, title, triggers_json,
   229	      importanceWeight, now, now, embeddingsProvider.getModelName(), now, embedding_status,
   230	      encodingIntent ?? 'document', documentType, specLevel, contentText, qualityScore, JSON.stringify(qualityFlags), parentId
   231	    );
   232	
   233	    const row_id = BigInt(result.lastInsertRowid);
   234	    const metadata_id = Number(row_id);
   235	
   236	    upsert_active_projection(database, specFolder, canonicalFilePath, anchorId, metadata_id, now);
   237	
   238	    if (sqlite_vec) {
   239	      // Remove orphaned vec_memories entry before insert
   240	      database.prepare('DELETE FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid = ?').run(row_id);
   241	      database.prepare(`
   242	        INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding) VALUES (?, ?)
   243	      `).run(row_id, embedding_buffer);
   244	    }
   245	
   246	    refresh_interference_scores_for_folder(database, specFolder);
   247	    // H3 FIX: Invalidate search cache after insert
   248	    clear_search_cache();
   249	
   250	    return metadata_id;
   251	  });
   252	
   253	  return index_memory_tx();
   254	}
   255	
   256	// Deferred indexing - entry searchable via BM25/FTS5 only
   257	/**
   258	 * Indexes a memory record without storing an embedding yet.
   259	 * @param params - The deferred memory values to index.
   260	 * @returns The indexed memory identifier.
   261	 */
   262	export function index_memory_deferred(
   263	  params: IndexMemoryDeferredParams,
   264	  database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
   265	): number {
   266	  const {
   267	    specFolder,
   268	    filePath,
   269	    parentId = null,
   270	    anchorId = null,
   271	    title = null,
   272	    triggerPhrases = [],
   273	    importanceWeight = 0.5,
   274	    failureReason = null,
   275	    encodingIntent,
   276	    documentType = 'memory',
   277	    specLevel = null,
   278	    contentText = null,
   279	    qualityScore = 0,
   280	    qualityFlags = [],

codex
A deeper pattern is starting to show: Engram’s local hygiene counters may not be fully portable. I’m tracing the sync payload shape now, because if `duplicate_count` or `revision_count` don’t survive import cleanly, that changes whether we should treat those counters as durable architecture or just local conveniences.

  2662	func (s *Store) backfillSessionSyncMutationsTx(tx *sql.Tx, project string) error {
  2663		rows, err := s.queryItHook(tx, `
  2664			SELECT id, project, directory, ended_at, summary
  2665			FROM sessions
  2666			WHERE project = ?
  2667			  AND NOT EXISTS (
  2668				SELECT 1
  2669				FROM sync_mutations sm
  2670				WHERE sm.target_key = ?
  2671				  AND sm.entity = ?
  2672				  AND sm.entity_key = sessions.id
  2673				  AND sm.source = ?
  2674			  )
  2675			ORDER BY started_at ASC, id ASC`,
  2676			project, DefaultSyncTargetKey, SyncEntitySession, SyncSourceLocal,
  2677		)
  2678		if err != nil {
  2679			return err
  2680		}
  2681		defer rows.Close()
  2682	
  2683		for rows.Next() {
  2684			var payload syncSessionPayload
  2685			if err := rows.Scan(&payload.ID, &payload.Project, &payload.Directory, &payload.EndedAt, &payload.Summary); err != nil {
  2686				return err
  2687			}
  2688			if err := s.enqueueSyncMutationTx(tx, SyncEntitySession, payload.ID, SyncOpUpsert, payload); err != nil {
  2689				return err
  2690			}
  2691		}
  2692		return rows.Err()
  2693	}
  2694

  2707				  AND sm.source = ?
  2708			  )
  2709			ORDER BY id ASC`,
  2710			project, DefaultSyncTargetKey, SyncEntityObservation, SyncSourceLocal,
  2711		)

   302	
   303	/**
   304	 * Provides the recordHistory helper.
   305	 */
   306	export function recordHistory(
   307	  memoryId: number,
   308	  event: 'ADD' | 'UPDATE' | 'DELETE',
   309	  prevValue: unknown,
   310	  newValue: unknown,
   311	  actor: string,
   312	  specFolder?: string | null,
   313	): string {
   314	  const database = getDatabase();
   315	  const id = generateUuid();
   316	  const prevValueText = normalizeHistoryValue(prevValue);
   317	  const newValueText = normalizeHistoryValue(newValue);
   318	  const normalizedActor = actor.trim() || 'system';
   319	  const resolvedSpecFolder = resolveSpecFolder(memoryId, specFolder);
   320	
   321	  if (!insertHistoryStatement) {
   322	    insertHistoryStatement = database.prepare(`
   323	      INSERT INTO memory_history (id, memory_id, event, prev_value, new_value, actor, spec_folder)
   324	      VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   325	    `) as Database.Statement;
   326	  }
   327	
   328	  insertHistoryStatement.run(
   329	    id,
   330	    memoryId,
   331	    event,
   332	    prevValueText,
   333	    newValueText,
   334	    normalizedActor,
   335	    resolvedSpecFolder,
   336	  );
   337	
   338	  return id;
   339	}
   340	
   341	/**
   342	 * Provides the getHistory helper.
   343	 */
   344	export function getHistory(memoryId: number, limit?: number): HistoryEntry[] {
   345	  const database = getDatabase();
   346	
   347	  const sql = limit != null
   348	    ? 'SELECT * FROM memory_history WHERE memory_id = ? ORDER BY timestamp DESC, rowid DESC LIMIT ?'
   349	    : 'SELECT * FROM memory_history WHERE memory_id = ? ORDER BY timestamp DESC, rowid DESC';
   350	
   351	  const params: (number)[] = limit != null ? [memoryId, limit] : [memoryId];
   352	
   353	  return database.prepare(sql).all(...params) as HistoryEntry[];
   354	}

   360	  memoryId: number,

[← Back to README](../README.md)

# Architecture

- [How It Works](#how-it-works)
- [Session Lifecycle](#session-lifecycle)
- [MCP Tools](#mcp-tools)
- [Progressive Disclosure](#progressive-disclosure-3-layer-pattern)
- [Memory Hygiene](#memory-hygiene)
- [Topic Key Workflow](#topic-key-workflow-recommended)
- [Project Structure](#project-structure)
- [CLI Reference](#cli-reference)

---

## How It Works

<p align="center">
  <img src="../assets/agent-save.png" alt="Agent saving a memory via mem_save" width="800" />
  <br />
  <em>The agent proactively calls <code>mem_save</code> after significant work — structured, searchable, no noise.</em>
</p>

Engram trusts the **agent** to decide what's worth remembering — not a firehose of raw tool calls.

### The Agent Saves, Engram Stores

```
1. Agent completes significant work (bugfix, architecture decision, etc.)
2. Agent calls mem_save with a structured summary:
   - title: "Fixed N+1 query in user list"
   - type: "bugfix"
   - content: What/Why/Where/Learned format
3. Engram persists to SQLite with FTS5 indexing
4. Next session: agent searches memory, gets relevant context
```

---

## Session Lifecycle

```
Session starts → Agent works → Agent saves memories proactively
                                    ↓
Session ends → Agent writes session summary (Goal/Discoveries/Accomplished/Files)
                                    ↓
Next session starts → Previous session context is injected automatically
```

---

## MCP Tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `mem_save` | Save a structured observation (decision, bugfix, pattern, etc.) |
| `mem_update` | Update an existing observation by ID |
| `mem_delete` | Delete an observation (soft-delete by default, hard-delete optional) |
| `mem_suggest_topic_key` | Suggest a stable `topic_key` for evolving topics before saving |
| `mem_search` | Full-text search across all memories |
| `mem_session_summary` | Save end-of-session summary |
| `mem_context` | Get recent context from previous sessions |
| `mem_timeline` | Chronological context around a specific observation |
| `mem_get_observation` | Get full content of a specific memory |
| `mem_save_prompt` | Save a user prompt for future context |
| `mem_stats` | Memory system statistics |
| `mem_session_start` | Register a session start |
| `mem_session_end` | Mark a session as completed |
| `mem_capture_passive` | Extract learnings from text output |
| `mem_merge_projects` | Merge project name variants into canonical name (admin) |

---

## Progressive Disclosure (3-Layer Pattern)

Token-efficient memory retrieval — don't dump everything, drill in:

```
1. mem_search "auth middleware"     → compact results with IDs (~100 tokens each)
2. mem_timeline observation_id=42  → what happened before/after in that session
3. mem_get_observation id=42       → full untruncated content
```

---

## Memory Hygiene

- `mem_save` now supports `scope` (`project` default, `personal` optional)
- `mem_save` also supports `topic_key`; with a topic key, saves become upserts (same project+scope+topic updates the existing memory)
- Exact dedupe prevents repeated inserts in a rolling window (hash + project + scope + type + title)
- Duplicates update metadata (`duplicate_count`, `last_seen_at`, `updated_at`) instead of creating new rows
- Topic upserts increment `revision_count` so evolving decisions stay in one memory
- `mem_delete` uses soft-delete by default (`deleted_at`), with optional hard delete
- `mem_search`, `mem_context`, recent lists, and timeline ignore soft-deleted observations

---

## Topic Key Workflow (Recommended)

Use this when a topic evolves over time (architecture, long-running feature decisions, etc.):

```text
1. mem_suggest_topic_key(type="architecture", title="Auth architecture")
2. mem_save(..., topic_key="architecture-auth-architecture")
3. Later change on same topic -> mem_save(..., same topic_key)
   => existing observation is updated (revision_count++)
```

Different topics should use different keys (e.g. `architecture/auth-model` vs `bug/auth-nil-panic`) so they never overwrite each other.

`mem_suggest_topic_key` now applies a family heuristic for consistency across sessions:

- `architecture/*` for architecture/design/ADR-like changes
- `bug/*` for fixes, regressions, errors, panics
- `decision/*`, `pattern/*`, `config/*`, `discovery/*`, `learning/*` when detected

---

## Project Structure

```
engram/
├── cmd/engram/main.go              # CLI entrypoint
├── internal/
│   ├── store/store.go              # Core: SQLite + FTS5 + all data ops
│   ├── server/server.go            # HTTP REST API (port 7437)
│   ├── mcp/mcp.go                  # MCP stdio server (15 tools)
│   ├── setup/setup.go              # Agent plugin installer (go:embed)
│   ├── project/                     # Project name detection + similarity matching
│   │   └── project.go              # DetectProject, FindSimilar, Levenshtein
│   ├── sync/sync.go                # Git sync: manifest + compressed chunks
│   └── tui/                        # Bubbletea terminal UI
│       ├── model.go                # Screen constants, Model, Init()
│       ├── styles.go               # Lipgloss styles (Catppuccin Mocha)
│       ├── update.go               # Input handling, per-screen handlers
│       └── view.go                 # Rendering, per-screen views
├── plugin/
│   ├── opencode/engram.ts          # OpenCode adapter plugin
│   └── claude-code/                # Claude Code plugin (hooks + skill)
│       ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│       ├── .mcp.json
│       ├── hooks/hooks.json
│       ├── scripts/                # session-start, post-compaction, subagent-stop, session-stop
│       └── skills/memory/SKILL.md
├── skills/                         # Contributor AI skills (repo-wide standards + Engram-specific guardrails)
├── setup.sh                        # Links repo skills into .claude/.codex/.gemini (project-local)
├── assets/                         # Screenshots and media
├── DOCS.md                         # Full technical documentation
├── CONTRIBUTING.md                 # Contribution workflow and standards
├── go.mod
└── go.sum
```

---

## CLI Reference

```
engram setup [agent]      Install/setup agent integration (opencode, claude-code, gemini-cli, codex)
engram serve [port]       Start HTTP API server (default: 7437)
engram mcp                Start MCP server (stdio transport)
engram tui                Launch interactive terminal UI
engram search <query>     Search memories
engram save <title> <msg> Save a memory
engram timeline <obs_id>  Chronological context around an observation
engram context [project]  Recent context from previous sessions
engram stats              Memory statistics
engram export [file]      Export all memories to JSON
engram import <file>      Import memories from JSON
engram sync               Export new memories as compressed chunk to .engram/
engram sync --all         Export ALL projects (ignore directory-based filter)
engram projects list      Show all projects with obs/session/prompt counts
engram projects consolidate  Interactive merge of similar project names [--all] [--dry-run]
engram projects prune     Remove projects with 0 observations [--dry-run]
engram version            Show version
```

### Finding 3: User prompts are a first-class continuity stream in Engram, not just summary material
- **Source**: [plugin/opencode/engram.ts#L324](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/specs\/system-spec-kit\/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade\/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems\/001-engram-main\/external\/plugin\/opencode\/engram.ts#L324), [internal/mcp/mcp.go#L350](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/specs\/system-spec-kit\/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade\/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems\/001-engram-main\/external\/internal\/mcp\/mcp.go#L350), [internal/store/store.go#L1109](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/specs\/system-spec-kit\/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade\/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems\/001-engram-main\/external\/internal\/store\/store.go#L1109), [internal/store/store.go#L1145](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/specs\/system-spec-kit\/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade\/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems\/001-engram-main\/external\/internal\/store\/store.go#L1145), [internal/store/store.go#L1613](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/specs\/system-spec-kit\/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade\/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems\/001-engram-main\/external\/internal\/store\/store.go#L1613), [scripts/memory/generate-context.ts#L75](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/skill\/system-spec-kit\/scripts\/memory\/generate-context.ts#L75)
- **What it does**: Every non-trivial user message is captured through `chat.message` into `user_prompts`; MCP also exposes `mem_save_prompt`; `FormatContext()` includes recent prompts as a separate section. This means Engram preserves user intent as its own lifecycle artifact instead of expecting future sessions to infer it from a final summary.
- **Why it matters for us**: In the Public code examined here, prompt preservation is centered on JSON-primary `generate-context` payloads and resume synthesis, not a dedicated always-on prompt ledger. If we want stronger “what was the user actually asking for?” continuity, this is one of Engram’s more useful ideas.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 4: Engram’s resume surface is intentionally lightweight, but too shallow to be our authoritative recovery model
- **Source**: [internal/server/server.go#L473](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/specs\/system-spec-kit\/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade\/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems\/001-engram-main\/external\/internal\/server\/server.go#L473), [internal/mcp/mcp.go#L883](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/specs\/system-spec-kit\/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade\/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems\/001-engram-main\/external\/internal\/mcp\/mcp.go#L883), [internal/store/store.go#L1613](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/specs\/system-spec-kit\/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade\/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems\/001-engram-main\/external\/internal\/store\/store.go#L1613), [handlers/session-resume.ts#L400](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/skill\/system-spec-kit\/mcp_server\/handlers\/session-resume.ts#L400), [handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/skill\/system-spec-kit\/mcp_server\/handlers\/session-bootstrap.ts#L163)
- **What it does**: Engram’s `mem_context` is just formatted output of recent sessions, recent prompts, recent observations, plus stats. It has no spec-folder contract, no structural readiness, no semantic-index status, and no bounded next-action guidance. It is a convenience context packet, not a composite recovery contract.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has the stronger pattern: `session_resume` merges resume memory, code-graph state, CocoIndex availability, and additive cached continuity; `session_bootstrap` adds health and recommended actions. Engram’s context format is fine as cheap startup seasoning, but it would be a regression as a primary recovery surface.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Engram’s compaction survival remains host-driven and ungated at the point of reuse
- **Source**: [plugin/opencode/engram.ts#L397](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/specs\/system-spec-kit\/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade\/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems\/001-engram-main\/external\/plugin\/opencode\/engram.ts#L397), [plugin/opencode/engram.ts#L423](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/specs\/system-spec-kit\/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade\/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems\/001-engram-main\/external\/plugin\/opencode\/engram.ts#L423), [internal/setup/setup.go#L104](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/specs\/system-spec-kit\/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade\/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems\/001-engram-main\/external\/internal\/setup\/setup.go#L104), [handlers/session-resume.ts#L300](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/skill\/system-spec-kit\/mcp_server\/handlers\/session-resume.ts#L300), [handlers/session-resume.ts#L479](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/skill\/system-spec-kit\/mcp_server\/handlers\/session-resume.ts#L479), [hooks/claude/session-stop.ts#L60](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/skill\/system-spec-kit\/mcp_server\/hooks\/claude\/session-stop.ts#L60), [hooks/claude/session-stop.ts#L299](\/Users\/michelkerkmeester\/MEGA\/Development\/Code_Environment\/Public\/.opencode\/skill\/system-spec-kit\/mcp_server\/hooks\/claude\/session-stop.ts#L299)
- **What it does**: During compaction, Engram fetches `/context` and appends it directly into the compaction payload, then injects prose telling the next agent to call `mem_session_summary`. Setup writes the same instruction family into Codex and Gemini install surfaces. There is no store-native checkpoint written at compaction time, and the reused continuity packet is not checked against scope/freshness/fingerprint gates before injection.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is the lifecycle fragility behind Engram’s otherwise useful session APIs. Public’s safer pattern is already visible in the code here: autosave flows through `generate-context`, while cached continuity is additive only after explicit scope and freshness checks in `session_resume`.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.69
- Questions addressed: Q2, Q6, Q7, Q9, Q10
- Questions answered: Q2, Q9, Q10
- Questions partially answered: Q6, Q7

## Reflection
- What worked: tracing `mem_session_summary` into `EndSession()`, `RecentSessions()`, and `FormatContext()` exposed the summary-authority split immediately; comparing that with Public’s `session_resume` acceptance gates and `session-stop` autosave path made the continuity tradeoff very clear.
- What did not work: CocoIndex MCP was not usable in this session, so this iteration relied on `rg` plus direct file reads; I also did not run the phase validator or memory-save command in this read-only research pass.

## Recommended Next Focus
Trace Engram’s sync and import path for session artifacts end to end, especially [internal/store/store.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go) around `applySessionPayloadTx()` and the sync tables, then compare that against Public’s shared-memory and governed-scope boundaries to see whether session, prompt, and summary fidelity survives cross-machine movement.

RESEARCH BRIEF:
# $refine TIDD-EC Prompt: 001-engram-main

## 2. Role

You are a research specialist in persistent memory architectures for AI agents, MCP server design, SQLite/FTS5 search systems, and agent-agnostic tool surfaces. Work like a systems analyst who can trace Go implementation details, separate durable patterns from packaging noise, and turn Engram's design into concrete improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`. Keep the analysis grounded in actual repository evidence, especially around tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic-key stability, passive capture, and search behavior.

## 3. Task

Research Engram's persistent memory architecture, MCP tool design, and session lifecycle patterns to identify practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially for Spec Kit Memory. Focus on how Engram separates agent tools from admin tools, persists session history, stabilizes evolving topics with `topic_key`, and ranks or shortcuts search using SQLite + FTS5. Compare Engram's choices against current Public capabilities and classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`. Prioritize improvements that strengthen session continuity, memory hygiene, search usefulness, and agent-agnostic integration without duplicating systems Public already has.

## 4. Context

### System Description

Engram is a single Go binary that exposes one persistent memory backend through four surfaces: CLI, HTTP API, MCP stdio, and an interactive TUI. The storage layer lives in SQLite with FTS5 virtual tables for observations and prompts, while the MCP layer in `internal/mcp/mcp.go` registers tools with explicit profile separation and usage guidance. The value proposition is operational simplicity: one binary, one local database, zero external runtime dependency, and compatibility across Claude Code, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Codex, VS Code Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, and other MCP-capable agents.

The core memory model is project-scoped and session-aware. `internal/store/store.go` persists sessions, observations, and user prompts separately; `CreateSession()` and `EndSession()` maintain session boundaries, while `FormatContext()` composes recent sessions, prompts, and observations into startup context. Session summaries are treated as first-class memory artifacts rather than free-form chat residue, and passive capture exists as a distinct pathway for extracting learnings from output text.

Engram also has a strong opinion on memory hygiene. `AddObservation()` supports `topic_key` upserts so evolving topics revise a stable record instead of generating fragment spam, while exact-duplicate detection increments `duplicate_count` inside a rolling window. `SuggestTopicKey()` assigns family prefixes such as `architecture/`, `bug/`, `decision/`, and `session/`, and `Search()` first checks direct topic-key matches before FTS5 `MATCH`, then orders full-text results by `fts.rank` after `sanitizeFTS()` quotes individual terms. This phase should treat those behaviors as the domain center of gravity, not the TUI.

### Cross-Phase Awareness Table

| Phase | System | Core Pattern | Overlap Risk | Differentiation |
|-------|--------|-------------|-------------|-----------------|
| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 003 (MCP memory), 004 (hybrid search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
| 002 | Mex | Markdown scaffold + drift detection | 001 (memory persistence) | Focus drift detection, scaffold structure, no-DB approach |
| 003 | Modus Memory | FSRS spaced repetition + BM25 | 001 (FTS5 search), 004 (local memory) | Focus FSRS decay, cross-referencing, librarian expansion |
| 004 | OpenCode Mnemosyne | Hybrid search (FTS5 + vector) | 001 (FTS5), 003 (local memory) | Focus vector search, OpenCode plugin architecture, scoping |

### What This Repo Already Has

`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with semantic retrieval via `memory_search`, session continuity via `memory_context`, trigger-based recall via `memory_match_triggers`, causal links, health/status tooling, and context save flows through `generate-context.js`. It also has CocoIndex for semantic code search and code-graph tooling for structural queries. What it does **not** currently have is a first-class session lifecycle API comparable to `mem_session_start` / `mem_session_end`, nor a lightweight MCP tool-profile split equivalent to Engram's `ProfileAgent` versus `ProfileAdmin`.

## 6. Research Questions

1. How effective is Engram's `ProfileAgent` versus `ProfileAdmin` split, and would a similar separation reduce tool clutter or accidental misuse in Public's memory surfaces?
2. What does Engram gain from explicit session lifecycle tools such as `mem_session_start`, `mem_session_end`, and `mem_session_summary`, and where would that improve Public beyond today's session continuity model?
3. How does `SuggestTopicKey()` create stable topic families, and could Public benefit from comparable topic-key or thread-key conventions for evolving decisions and repeated refinements?

codex
# Iteration 006: MEMORY HYGIENE

## Focus
MEMORY HYGIENE: Deduplication, upserts, revision tracking, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Engram cleanly separates “same topic evolved” from “same content repeated,” but only inside a single live row
- **Source**: [store.go:948](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L948), [store.go:1013](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1013), [create-record.ts:244](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/create-record.ts#L244), [memory-save.ts:937](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L937), [history.ts:306](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/history.ts#L306)
- **What it does**: `AddObservation()` first checks `topic_key` and rewrites the newest matching row, incrementing `revision_count`; only if no topic match exists does it run rolling-window duplicate detection by normalized hash plus project/scope/type/title, incrementing `duplicate_count` instead of inserting a new row. Public’s save path is different: unchanged same-path saves short-circuit, cross-path hash matches are rejected, and real revisions create new append-first versions plus history/lineage edges rather than mutating one row.
- **Why it matters for us**: Engram has a useful distinction between revision and recurrence, but its counters live on the current row only. Public already has stronger revision auditability; what we are missing is the lightweight “this exact thing resurfaced again” signal.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: Engram’s hygiene counters are durable in export/import, but not durable in mutation sync
- **Source**: [store.go:208](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L208), [store.go:1766](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1766), [store.go:2872](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L2872), [store.go:2900](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L2900), [store.go:2076](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L2076)
- **What it does**: Full JSON import preserves `revision_count`, `duplicate_count`, and `deleted_at`. But the incremental sync payload omits both counters entirely; `applyObservationUpsertTx()` recreates rows with `revision_count=1`, `duplicate_count=1`, and on updates simply does `revision_count = revision_count + 1` while clearing `deleted_at`. So local counters survive export/import, but sync replication treats them as incidental.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is the biggest new hygiene caveat in Engram. Its counters are not trustworthy distributed truth after cross-machine sync, so they are weak foundations for shared-memory analytics or merge policy. Public’s append-first lineage and explicit history are safer source-of-truth primitives.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Engram’s soft delete is a real tombstone model with undelete-by-upsert, but its garbage collection is intentionally shallow
- **Source**: [store.go:450](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L450), [store.go:1313](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1313), [store.go:1462](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462), [store.go:2913](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L2913), [store.go:2520](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L2520), [memory-crud-delete.ts:91](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-delete.ts#L91), [vector-index-mutations.ts:516](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts#L516), [archival-manager.ts:558](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L558)
- **What it does**: Engram writes `deleted_at`, excludes those rows from search/context/recent lists, emits delete sync payloads, and can effectively undelete the row later because sync upsert forces `deleted_at = NULL`. But there is no automatic purge of soft-deleted observations; `PruneProject()` only deletes prompts and sessions once live observation count is zero. Public’s deletion model is the inverse: `memory_delete` is physical row deletion, while reversible lifecycle management lives in `is_archived` plus explicit archive/unarchive flows.
- **Why it matters for us**: Engram’s tombstone semantics are attractive for future shared-memory restore/conflict flows, but not as a general garbage-collection strategy. Public is already stronger on actual cleanup and reversible archival separation.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 4: Engram’s `topic_key` is semantic identity; Public’s logical key is storage identity
- **Source**: [store.go:3198](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3198), [mcp.go:53](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L53), [mcp.go:302](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L302), [lineage-state.ts:207](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L207), [lineage-state.ts:232](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L232), [lineage-state.ts:251](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L251)
- **What it does**: Engram derives a stable semantic family like `architecture/*`, `bug/*`, `decision/*`, then normalizes title/content into a reusable segment for cross-session upserts. Public’s logical identity is built from scope hash plus canonical path plus anchor, with hashed fallback only to preserve structural uniqueness.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public is excellent at “which stored artifact/version is active?” but weaker at “this is the same evolving decision thread even if it moved files or changed document boundaries.” Engram’s semantic key is a useful complement, not a replacement.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Public already exceeds Engram on revision auditability and lifecycle cleanup, so Engram should only influence metadata ergonomics
- **Source**: [store.go:980](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L980), [store.go:1277](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1277), [create-record.ts:253](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/create-record.ts#L253), [lineage-state.ts:666](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L666), [lineage-state.ts:1257](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L1257), [history.ts:306](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/history.ts#L306), [session-manager.ts:771](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/session/session-manager.ts#L771), [archival-manager.ts:371](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L371)
- **What it does**: Engram tracks revision with scalar counters on a mutable current row. Public records transition edges, preserves history events, marks predecessors deprecated, archives separately, and cleans stale session-state/working-memory artifacts on a timer.
- **Why it matters for us**: The right synthesis is narrow. Borrow Engram’s semantic thread-key idea and maybe a recurrence counter, but do not collapse Public’s append-first lineage and cleanup model into single-row revision integers.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

## Reflection
- What worked: starting at `AddObservation()`, then following delete/import/sync paths, exposed the real hygiene model quickly. The most valuable step was tracing `syncObservationPayload` into `applyObservationUpsertTx()`, which showed that Engram’s counters are not fully sync-authoritative even though export/import preserves them.
- What did not work: CocoIndex MCP and Spec Kit Memory MCP both failed in this session, so this pass relied on `rg` plus direct file reads. I also kept this iteration read-only and did not run the spec validator or save memory.

## Recommended Next Focus
Trace project merge and sync collision behavior next: `mem_merge_projects`, `NormalizeProject()`, sync chunk import, and any project-name consolidation paths that can reshape `topic_key` neighborhoods. The key question is whether Engram’s semantic threading stays stable when project identities drift or merge across machines.

# Iteration 006: MEMORY HYGIENE

## Focus
MEMORY HYGIENE: Deduplication, upserts, revision tracking, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Engram cleanly separates “same topic evolved” from “same content repeated,” but only inside a single live row
- **Source**: [store.go:948](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L948), [store.go:1013](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1013), [create-record.ts:244](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/create-record.ts#L244), [memory-save.ts:937](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L937), [history.ts:306](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/history.ts#L306)
- **What it does**: `AddObservation()` first checks `topic_key` and rewrites the newest matching row, incrementing `revision_count`; only if no topic match exists does it run rolling-window duplicate detection by normalized hash plus project/scope/type/title, incrementing `duplicate_count` instead of inserting a new row. Public’s save path is different: unchanged same-path saves short-circuit, cross-path hash matches are rejected, and real revisions create new append-first versions plus history/lineage edges rather than mutating one row.
- **Why it matters for us**: Engram has a useful distinction between revision and recurrence, but its counters live on the current row only. Public already has stronger revision auditability; what we are missing is the lightweight “this exact thing resurfaced again” signal.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: Engram’s hygiene counters are durable in export/import, but not durable in mutation sync
- **Source**: [store.go:208](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L208), [store.go:1766](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1766), [store.go:2872](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L2872), [store.go:2900](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L2900), [store.go:2076](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L2076)
- **What it does**: Full JSON import preserves `revision_count`, `duplicate_count`, and `deleted_at`. But the incremental sync payload omits both counters entirely; `applyObservationUpsertTx()` recreates rows with `revision_count=1`, `duplicate_count=1`, and on updates simply does `revision_count = revision_count + 1` while clearing `deleted_at`. So local counters survive export/import, but sync replication treats them as incidental.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is the biggest new hygiene caveat in Engram. Its counters are not trustworthy distributed truth after cross-machine sync, so they are weak foundations for shared-memory analytics or merge policy. Public’s append-first lineage and explicit history are safer source-of-truth primitives.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Engram’s soft delete is a real tombstone model with undelete-by-upsert, but its garbage collection is intentionally shallow
- **Source**: [store.go:450](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L450), [store.go:1313](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1313), [store.go:1462](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462), [store.go:2913](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L2913), [store.go:2520](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L2520), [memory-crud-delete.ts:91](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-delete.ts#L91), [vector-index-mutations.ts:516](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts#L516), [archival-manager.ts:558](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L558)
- **What it does**: Engram writes `deleted_at`, excludes those rows from search/context/recent lists, emits delete sync payloads, and can effectively undelete the row later because sync upsert forces `deleted_at = NULL`. But there is no automatic purge of soft-deleted observations; `PruneProject()` only deletes prompts and sessions once live observation count is zero. Public’s deletion model is the inverse: `memory_delete` is physical row deletion, while reversible lifecycle management lives in `is_archived` plus explicit archive/unarchive flows.
- **Why it matters for us**: Engram’s tombstone semantics are attractive for future shared-memory restore/conflict flows, but not as a general garbage-collection strategy. Public is already stronger on actual cleanup and reversible archival separation.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 4: Engram’s `topic_key` is semantic identity; Public’s logical key is storage identity
- **Source**: [store.go:3198](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3198), [mcp.go:53](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L53), [mcp.go:302](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L302), [lineage-state.ts:207](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L207), [lineage-state.ts:232](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L232), [lineage-state.ts:251](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L251)
- **What it does**: Engram derives a stable semantic family like `architecture/*`, `bug/*`, `decision/*`, then normalizes title/content into a reusable segment for cross-session upserts. Public’s logical identity is built from scope hash plus canonical path plus anchor, with hashed fallback only to preserve structural uniqueness.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public is excellent at “which stored artifact/version is active?” but weaker at “this is the same evolving decision thread even if it moved files or changed document boundaries.” Engram’s semantic key is a useful complement, not a replacement.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Public already exceeds Engram on revision auditability and lifecycle cleanup, so Engram should only influence metadata ergonomics
- **Source**: [store.go:980](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L980), [store.go:1277](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1277), [create-record.ts:253](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/create-record.ts#L253), [lineage-state.ts:666](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L666), [lineage-state.ts:1257](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L1257), [history.ts:306](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/history.ts#L306), [session-manager.ts:771](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/session/session-manager.ts#L771), [archival-manager.ts:371](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L371)
- **What it does**: Engram tracks revision with scalar counters on a mutable current row. Public records transition edges, preserves history events, marks predecessors deprecated, archives separately, and cleans stale session-state/working-memory artifacts on a timer.
- **Why it matters for us**: The right synthesis is narrow. Borrow Engram’s semantic thread-key idea and maybe a recurrence counter, but do not collapse Public’s append-first lineage and cleanup model into single-row revision integers.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.78
- Questions addressed: Q3, Q4, Q8, Q9, Q10
- Questions answered: Q3, Q4, Q9, Q10
- Questions partially answered: Q8

## Reflection
- What worked: starting at `AddObservation()`, then following delete/import/sync paths, exposed the real hygiene model quickly. The most valuable step was tracing `syncObservationPayload` into `applyObservationUpsertTx()`, which showed that Engram’s counters are not fully sync-authoritative even though export/import preserves them.
- What did not work: CocoIndex MCP and Spec Kit Memory MCP both failed in this session, so this pass relied on `rg` plus direct file reads. I also kept this iteration read-only and did not run the spec validator or save memory.

## Recommended Next Focus
Trace project merge and sync collision behavior next: `mem_merge_projects`, `NormalizeProject()`, sync chunk import, and any project-name consolidation paths that can reshape `topic_key` neighborhoods. The key question is whether Engram’s semantic threading stays stable when project identities drift or merge across machines.
